PLW Apprentice Final Merit List 2026 OUT (Direct Link) - Download

Quick Summary: Patiala Locomotive Works (PLW), Patiala has released PLW Apprentice Final Merit List 2026 on 08 April 2026 at official portal plw.indianrailways.gov.in. Candidates can check the final merit list and cut off marks. Report for joining by 20 April 2026. Check selected candidates list, cut off, and important instructions below.

Are you waiting for PLW Apprentice Final Merit List 2026? Great news! Patiala Locomotive Works (PLW) has officially published the Final Merit List & Cut Off for Act Apprentice 2025-26 (08 April 2026). Candidates who appeared in the selection process for various ITI trades can now check their final selection status online.

This article provides complete information about PLW Apprentice Final Merit List 2026 including direct download links, cut off marks, joining schedule, and step-by-step instructions to check the merit list.

PLW Apprentice 2025-26 - Result Dashboard

Particulars Details
Recruiting Body Patiala Locomotive Works (PLW), Indian Railways
Post Name Act Apprentice (Various ITI Trades)
Result Type Final Merit List & Cut Off
Result Declaration Date 08 April 2026
Joining / Reporting Date By 20 April 2026 at PLW Patiala
Selection Stages Merit Based on ITI Marks → Document Verification → Final Merit List
Official Website plw.indianrailways.gov.in

Check Your Final Merit List Now

Download PLW Apprentice Final Merit List 2026

Direct official link - Final Merit List & Cut Off PDF

How to Check PLW Apprentice Final Merit List 2026?

Candidates can download the PLW Apprentice Final Merit List 2026 by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official PLW website: plw.indianrailways.gov.in
  2. Go to the "Engagement of Act Apprentices" or "News & Announcement" section
  3. Click on the link – "Final Merit List and Cut Off for Act Apprentices 2025-26"
  4. Download the PDF file
  5. Search your name or roll number using Ctrl + F
  6. Check your position and cut off marks for your trade/category
  7. Save and take printout for joining purpose

PLW Apprentice Final Merit List 2026 - What's Inside?

The PLW Apprentice Final Merit List 2026 contains details of all finally selected candidates for apprenticeship training in various ITI trades. The list is prepared on the basis of ITI marks and document verification.

Merit List Contains:

  • Serial Number
  • Candidate Name
  • Father’s Name
  • Trade Name
  • Category (UR/OBC/SC/ST/EWS)
  • ITI Marks / Percentage
  • Final Merit Position
  • Cut Off Marks (Trade & Category wise)
  • Remarks (Selected / Waiting)

Types of Lists Released:

  1. Final Merit List: Main selected candidates for joining
  2. Waiting List: Candidates in reserve for future vacancies
  3. Trade-wise & Category-wise Cut Off: Minimum qualifying marks

PLW Apprentice Cut Off 2026 - Important Information

Cut off marks have been released along with the final merit list. Cut off varies for different trades and categories. Candidates are advised to check the official PDF for exact cut off of their trade.

Important Instructions for Selected Candidates

  • ✓ Report to PLW Patiala by 20 April 2026 without fail
  • ✓ Carry all original documents and 2 sets of photocopies
  • ✓ Download and print the final merit list PDF
  • ✓ Complete medical examination as per schedule
  • ✓ Verify all personal and academic details immediately
  • ✓ Keep checking official website for any updates
  • ✓ Join training on the given date

Final Cut Off Marks Available Download PDF

Are you waiting for UPSC NDA II Final Result 2026? Great news! The Union Public Service Commission has officially published the final result for National Defence Academy & Naval Academy Examination (II) 2025 (08 April 2026). Thousands of candidates who appeared in the written examination held on 14 September 2025 and cleared SSB Interview across various centres can now verify their final selection status online.

This article provides complete information about UPSC NDA II Final Result 2026 including direct download links, merit list, selection details, and step-by-step instructions to check results.

UPSC NDA II 2025 - Result Dashboard

Particulars Details

Exam Name National Defence Academy & Naval Academy Examination (II) 2025
Result Type Final Result (Written + SSB Interview)
Result Declaration Date 08 April 2026

Selection Stages Written Test → SSB Interview → Medical Examination → Document Verification & Joining
Official Website upsc.gov.in

Download UPSC NDA II Final Result 2026

How to Check UPSC NDA II Final Result 2026?

Candidates can download the UPSC NDA II Final Result 2026 merit list by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official UPSC website: upsc.gov.in
  2. Go to the "What's New" or "Examinations" section
  3. Click on the link – "Final Result: National Defence Academy and Naval Academy Examination (II), 2025"
  4. Download the PDF file
  5. Search your Roll Number or Name using Ctrl + F
  6. Save and take printout for future reference

UPSC NDA II Merit List 2026 - What's Inside?

The UPSC NDA II Final Merit List 2026 contains details of all finally selected candidates for admission to NDA (Army, Navy, Air Force) and Naval Academy. UPSC prepares separate merit lists based on written exam + SSB performance.

Merit List Contains:

  • Roll Numbers of finally recommended candidates
  • Candidate Names
  • Service Allocation (Army / Navy / Air Force / Naval Academy)
  • Final Merit Position
  • Qualifying Status

Types of Merit Lists Released:

  1. Final Merit List: Overall recommended candidates for all wings
  2. Service-wise Allocation: Separate for Army, Navy, Air Force & Naval Academy
  3. Reserve List: Candidates kept in waiting if selected candidates do not join

UPSC NDA II Final Result 2026 - Important Instructions for Selected Candidates

  • ✓ Download and save the merit list PDF immediately
  • ✓ Take multiple printouts for future reference
  • ✓ Wait for joining instructions from Ministry of Defence / respective academies
  • ✓ Complete medical formalities as per schedule
  • ✓ Keep all original documents ready for verification
  • ✓ Verify personal details - report any discrepancy immediately
  • ✓ Marks of recommended candidates will be available on UPSC website after 15 days

Punjab PSTET Result 2026 OUT (Direct Link) - Download Scorecard

Quick Summary: State Council of Educational Research and Training (SCERT) Punjab on behalf of Punjab School Education Board (PSEB) has released Punjab PSTET Result 2026 on 7th April 2026 at official portal pstet2025.org. Candidates can download their scorecard for Paper 1 & Paper 2 using Registration Number & Password / Date of Birth. Check qualifying marks, merit list details, and next steps for eligibility certificate below.

Are you waiting for Punjab PSTET Result 2026? Great news! The Punjab State Teacher Eligibility Test (PSTET) 2026 result for Paper 1 (Classes I-V) and Paper 2 (Classes VI-VIII) has been officially declared today (7th April 2026). Lakhs of candidates who appeared in the examination held on 15th March 2026 across various centres in Punjab can now check their qualifying status and download scorecards online.

This article provides complete information about Punjab PSTET Result 2026 including direct download links, scorecard details, qualifying marks, and step-by-step instructions to check results.

Punjab PSTET 2026 - Result Dashboard

Particulars Details
Conducting Body SCERT Punjab / PSEB
Exam Name Punjab State Teacher Eligibility Test (PSTET) 2026
Result Type Paper 1 & Paper 2 Scorecard
Result Declaration Date 7th April 2026
Exam Conducted On 15th March 2026
Selection / Eligibility Qualifying Marks → Lifetime Valid TET Certificate
Official Website pstet2025.org

Check Your Result Now

Download PSTET Result 2026 Scorecard

Direct official link - Login with Registration Number & Password

How to Check Punjab PSTET Result 2026?

Candidates can download their PSTET 2026 scorecard by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official website: pstet2025.org
  2. Click on the “PSTET Result 2026” or “Candidate Login” link
  3. Enter your Registration Number and Password / Date of Birth
  4. Submit details to view your Paper 1 / Paper 2 marks and qualifying status
  5. Download and print the scorecard for future reference

Punjab PSTET Merit List 2026 - What’s Inside?

The PSTET merit list 2026 includes details of all qualified candidates. Separate qualifying lists are prepared for Paper 1 and Paper 2.

Scorecard / Merit List Contains:

  • Registration Number / Roll Number
  • Candidate Name
  • Father’s Name
  • Category (General / OBC / SC / ST / EWS)
  • Subject-wise Marks (Paper 1 or Paper 2)
  • Total Marks Obtained (Out of 150)
  • Qualifying Status (Qualified / Not Qualified)
  • Overall Rank (if applicable)

Qualifying Marks:

  • General Category: 90 marks (60%)
  • Reserved Categories: 82 marks (55%)

Qualified candidates will receive a lifetime-valid TET eligibility certificate for teacher recruitment in Punjab.

Punjab PSTET Scorecard 2026 - Information Breakdown

Your PSTET scorecard 2026 will display the following information:

Candidate Name As per application form
Registration Number Unique login ID
Exam Date 15th March 2026
Paper Type Paper 1 or Paper 2
Subject-wise Marks Child Development, Language, Mathematics, EVS/Social Science etc.
Total Marks Obtained Out of 150
Qualifying Status Qualified / Not Qualified

Important Instructions for Qualified Candidates

  • ✓ Download and save your scorecard immediately
  • ✓ Take multiple printouts for future reference
  • ✓ The TET certificate is valid for lifetime
  • ✓ Use the certificate for teacher recruitment applications in Punjab
  • ✓ Verify all details on scorecard - report discrepancies immediately
  • ✓ Keep registration details safe for future logins
  • ✓ Check official website regularly for any further updates

SSC CGL Final Result 2025 Out - 15118 Candidates Selected (First Round Tentative Allocation)

Quick Summary: Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has released SSC CGL 2025 First Round of Tentative Allocation (FRTA) on 8th April 2026 at official portal ssc.gov.in. 15,118 candidates have been provisionally shortlisted for various posts including Junior Statistical Officer (JSO), Statistical Investigator (SI) Grade-II and other Group B & C posts. Candidates can download List-1, List-2 & List-3 in PDF format. Check your status and complete Sliding Process immediately.

Congratulations to all selected candidates! The Staff Selection Commission has officially declared the SSC CGL Final Result 2025 (First Round Tentative Allocation) today on 8th April 2026. This allocation is based on performance in Tier-II Examination (Section-I & Section-II), Computer Knowledge Test (CKT) and Data Entry Speed Test (DEST).

This article provides complete information about SSC CGL 2025 Result including direct download links, merit lists, important instructions for Sliding Process, next steps, and more.

How to Check SSC CGL Final Result 2025?

Follow these simple steps to download your result:

  1. Visit the official website: https://ssc.gov.in/
  2. Click on the “Results” tab on the homepage
  3. Select “Combined Graduate Level (CGL) Examination, 2025”
  4. Click on the relevant list:
    • List-1: For Junior Statistical Officer (JSO) and Statistical Investigator (SI) Grade-II
    • List-2: For all other posts
    • List-3: Candidates withheld due to various reasons
  5. Download the PDF file
  6. Open the PDF and search your Roll Number using Ctrl + F
  7. Save and print the result for future reference

SSC CGL Final Cut Off 2025 - Category Wise (Overall / Other Posts)

The following table shows the minimum cut off marks (last selected candidate) for allocation in List-2 (other posts). Exact post-wise final cut offs vary slightly based on preferences and vacancies.

SSC CGL Merit List 2025 - What's Inside?

The SSC CGL 2025 FRTA Lists contain roll numbers of provisionally shortlisted candidates in roll number order. Final selection will be made after the Sliding Process and Document Verification.

Merit List Contains:

  • Roll Numbers of shortlisted candidates
  • Category-wise allocation details
  • Provisional selection status
  • Instructions for further process

Types of Lists Released:

  1. List-1: For JSO and Statistical Investigator (SI) Grade-II posts
  2. List-2: For all other posts (Income Tax Inspector, Assistant Section Officer, Auditor, etc.)
  3. List-3: Candidates withheld due to various reasons

Important Instructions for Shortlisted Candidates

  • ✓ Participate in the Sliding Process – Select venue/date/slot between 8th to 10th April 2026
  • ✓ Physically appear at the concerned Regional Office from 13th April 2026 onwards
  • ✓ Failure to attend the Sliding Process will result in exclusion from final allocation
  • ✓ Sliding for unutilized vacancies will continue from 13th to 22nd April 2026
  • ✓ Final appointment letters will be issued by the respective User Departments
  • ✓ Keep all original documents ready for verification
  • ✓ Regularly check ssc.gov.in for further updates

MEDA Result 2026 Out For Project Executive Officer, Project Officer and Accountant - Download

Quick Summary: Maharashtra Energy Development Agency (MEDA) has released MEDA Result 2026 on 8th April 2026 at official portal mahaurja.maharashtra.gov.in. Candidates can download Select List, Wait List & Consolidated Marks List for Project Executive Officer, Project Officer and Accountant posts. Check merit list, qualifying status, and next steps below.

Great news for candidates who appeared in the MEDA Recruitment 2026 examination! The Maharashtra Energy Development Agency (MEDA) has officially declared the MEDA Result 2026 today on 8th April 2026 for Advertisement No. 01/2025. Thousands of aspirants who participated in the Online Computer-Based Test (CBT) can now check their selection status, marks, and merit position online.

This article provides complete details about MEDA Result 2026 including direct links to merit lists, how to download PDFs, selection process, important instructions, and more.

MEDA Result 2026 - Result Dashboard

Particulars Details
Recruiting Body Maharashtra Energy Development Agency (MEDA)
Post Names Project Executive Officer, Project Officer, Accountant
Result Type Select List, Wait List & Consolidated Marks List
Result Declaration Date 8th April 2026
Exam Conducted On Online CBT (Date not specified in notification)
Selection Stages Online CBT → Document Verification → Final Merit
Official Website mahaurja.maharashtra.gov.in

Download MEDA Result 2026

Direct official links - Search by Roll Number/Name in PDFs

How to Check MEDA Result 2026?

Candidates can easily access the MEDA Result 2026 by following these steps:

  1. Visit the official website: https://www.mahaurja.maharashtra.gov.in
  2. Go to the “Recruitment 2025” or “What’s New” section
  3. Click on “Advertisement No. 01/2025 – Result Declaration” (dated 08/04/2026)
  4. Download the relevant PDF: Select List / Wait List / Consolidated Marks List for your post
  5. Open the PDF and use Ctrl + F to search your Roll Number or Name
  6. Download and save the files for future reference

MEDA Merit List 2026 - What's Inside?

The MEDA Merit List 2026 includes category-wise Select Lists and Wait Lists along with Consolidated Marks Lists for all three posts. Merit is prepared on the basis of normalized scores obtained in the CBT.

Merit List Contains:

  • Roll Number & Candidate Name
  • Category (General / Reserved)
  • Marks Obtained (Normalized)
  • Selection Status (Selected / Waitlisted)
  • Rank in Merit (where applicable)

Types of Lists Released:

  1. Select List: Main list of recommended candidates against vacancies
  2. Wait List: Additional candidates in order of merit
  3. Consolidated Marks List: Marks of all candidates who appeared

MEDA Scorecard / Marks List 2026 - Information Breakdown

The Consolidated Marks List displays the following details:

Candidate Details Roll Number, Name, Category
Exam Details Advertisement No. 01/2025
Marks Normalized Score (out of 200)
Qualifying Status Based on minimum 45% (40% for reserved categories)

Important Instructions for Selected / Waitlisted Candidates

  • ✓ Download and print all relevant lists immediately
  • ✓ The Select List is valid for 1 year from the date of preparation
  • ✓ Keep documents ready for any further verification if required
  • ✓ Regularly check the official website for joining updates
  • ✓ Report any discrepancy in marks/name to MEDA authorities promptly
  • ✓ Use Ctrl + F for quick search in PDF files

As per the latest updates, the final marks and scorecard were made available on February 17, 2026, at the official website ssc.gov.in. This follows the declaration of the SSC GD final result on January 15, 2026.

Candidates who appeared in the PST/PET and DV/DME/RME stages can now download their SSC GD final scorecard 2025 using their registration number and password. The scorecard displays normalized CBT marks and overall qualifying status after all recruitment stages. The download window is limited - typically a short period - so act quickly before the link deactivates.

Key Highlights:

  • Release Date: February 17, 2026
  • Last Date to Download: March 1, 2026 (till 6:00 PM)
  • Access: Online only via ssc.gov.in login
  • Purpose: View final marks, compare with cut-offs, and save for records/joining

Final Result Declaration January 15, 2026

Direct Link to Check SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Candidates can access the SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 through the direct link on the SSC portal under the “Result/Marks” section after logging in. The portal may redirect from legacy links, but use ssc.gov.in primarily.

SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 – Direct Link (Active)
(Login required at ssc.gov.in for marks download)

Step-by-Step Guide to Download SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

  1. Visit the official Staff Selection Commission website: ssc.gov.in.
  2. Click on the “Login” or “Candidate Login” button on the homepage.
  3. Enter your registration number/username, password, and captcha (if prompted).
  4. After logging in, navigate to the “Result/Marks” tab on your dashboard.
  5. Select “Constable (GD) in CAPFs, SSF, NIA and Rifleman (GD) in Assam Rifles Examination 2025” from the list.
  6. Click on the “SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025” or “Marks” link to view your details.
  7. Your final scorecard will display on screen - review marks, cut-off comparison, and status.
  8. Download the PDF and print it for future reference (e.g., document verification). Save it promptly, as access ends after the deadline.
  9. (Use the direct link mentioned above to reach the login faster if available.)

Details Mentioned on SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Your scorecard typically includes:

  • Candidate’s name, roll number, registration number
  • Category/sub-category (UR, OBC, SC, ST, EWS, etc.)
  • Exam name and applied post
  • Section-wise marks (General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Knowledge & General Awareness, Elementary Mathematics, English/Hindi)
  • Raw and normalized marks
  • Total marks in CBT (considered for final merit after qualifying PET/PST and medical)
  • Qualifying status (selected/not selected) and any remarks
  • Note on limited availability window

SSC GD Final Cut Off and Merit List 2025

The SSC GD final cut off 2025 was released with the final result on January 15, 2026, and is force-wise, state-wise, category-wise, and gender-wise. It appears in the official result PDF write-up.

Your scorecard marks should be compared to the relevant cut-off for your category/force/state to assess selection. The merit list PDF lists roll numbers of provisionally selected candidates (around 50,047 selected against vacancies), while the scorecard provides individual marks for those who reached physical/medical stages.

(Refer to the official SSC result PDFs for exact cut-off tables - examples range from ~104 to 152+ depending on category/force.)

What to Do After Downloading SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Selected candidates: Keep multiple printouts/PDFs of the scorecard and final result for document verification, joining, and force allocation.

Verify details (name, roll number, category, etc.) - report serious discrepancies to SSC regional offices promptly.

Non-selected candidates: Use marks for performance analysis and preparation for future SSC exams.

Download and save copies early - the window closes after the last date.

SSC GD Selection Process 2025 (Short Overview)

The process included:

  • Computer-Based Test (CBT): Four sections totaling 160 marks.
  • Physical Efficiency Test (PET) and Physical Standard Test (PST): Qualifying.
  • Detailed Medical Examination (DME)/Review Medical Examination (RME): Qualifying.
  • Final merit based on normalized CBT marks (for qualified candidates), followed by force/state allocation per vacancies and preferences.

The final scorecard reflects CBT performance post-qualifying stages, available briefly post-result.

Important Links for SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

  • SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 Download: ssc.gov.in (login to Result/Marks)
  • SSC GD Final Result 2025 Merit List PDF: Available on ssc.gov.in under Results
  • SSC GD Final Cut Off 2025 PDF: In final result write-up on ssc.gov.in
  • SSC Official Website: ssc.gov.in

How to Download JSSC Excise Constable Admit Card 2026?

Follow these simple steps to download your JSSC Excise Constable Admit Card 2026:

  1. Step 1: Visit the official JSSC website at jssc.jharkhand.gov.in or click any of the direct download links provided above
  2. Step 2: Navigate to the “Admit Card” or “Latest Announcements” section on the homepage

  6. Step 6: Your JSSC Excise Constable Admit Card 2026 will appear on screen

Pro Tip: Check all details on your admit card immediately after download. Report any discrepancies to JSSC helpdesk within 24 hours.

Details on JSSC Excise Constable Admit Card 2026

Your JSSC Excise Constable Admit Card 2026 will contain the following information:

Candidate Information:

  • Candidate Name
  • Father's Name
  • Date of Birth
  • Category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS)
  • Gender
  • Application/Roll Number
  • Candidate's Photograph and Signature

Exam Information:

  • Exam Name: Excise Constable Written Test
  • Exam Date & Shift/Timing
  • Reporting Time
  • Exam Center Name and Complete Address
  • Exam Center Code

Important Instructions:

  • Documents to carry on exam day
  • Items allowed and not allowed in examination hall
  • Exam day instructions and rules

Documents Required on JSSC Excise Constable Exam Day

Mandatory Documents (Originals):

  • JSSC Excise Constable Admit Card 2026 (printed copy with clear photograph)
  • Valid Photo ID Proof (any one of the following):
    • Aadhaar Card
    • Voter ID Card
    • Driving License
    • PAN Card
    • Passport
  • Recent passport-size photographs (2 copies - same as uploaded in application)
  • Simple pen (blue/black ballpoint)

Optional but Recommended:

  • Pencil and eraser (for rough work)
  • Water bottle (transparent, without label)
  • Hand sanitizer (small bottle)

Not Allowed in Examination Hall:

  • Mobile phones and smartphones
  • Smartwatches and digital watches
  • Calculators and electronic devices
  • Books, notes, and study materials
  • Bags and pouches
  • Bluetooth devices and earphones

Important Instructions for JSSC Excise Constable Exam 2026

Before Exam Day:

  • Download admit card immediately
  • Check exam center location on Google Maps
  • Plan your route and transportation
  • Arrange all required documents in advance
  • Keep backup copies of admit card
  • Read exam instructions carefully

On Exam Day:

  • Reach exam center 45-60 minutes before reporting time
  • Entry closes 30 minutes before exam starts (No late entry allowed)
  • Follow dress code if specified in admit card
  • Carry water and light snacks for outside exam hall
  • Keep calm and focused

During Exam:

  • Follow all invigilator instructions carefully
  • Fill OMR sheet with black/blue ballpoint pen only (if applicable)
  • Manage your time wisely
  • Don't panic if questions seem difficult
  • Review answers if time permits
  • Don't carry prohibited items inside exam hall

What If JSSC Excise Constable Admit Card Not Available?

If you’re unable to download your JSSC Excise Constable Admit Card 2026, try these solutions:

  1. Solution 1: Clear browser cache and cookies, then try again with a fresh session
  2. Solution 2: Try different web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Microsoft Edge, Safari)
  3. Solution 3: Use the direct CBT portal link: https://je23x7k9h4.cbtexam.in/Jssc/index.html
  4. Solution 4: Contact JSSC helpdesk immediately

Important: Act immediately if facing issues downloading admit card. Don’t wait until exam day. Contact JSSC helpdesk at least 2 days before the exam.

Bank of Baroda Peon Admit Card 2026 OUT - Download Hall Ticket at bankofbaroda.in

Bank of Baroda Peon Admit Card 2026 (Mains) has been released by Bank of Baroda on 12 March 2026. Candidates who qualified the preliminary exam for the Office Assistant (Peon) recruitment (Advt No. BOB/HRM/REC/ADVT/2025/05) can download their admit card from the official website bankofbaroda.in. The Online Mains examination is scheduled for 22 March 2026 across various centers in India. This hall ticket is mandatory for appearing in the exam. Read below for the direct download link, step-by-step process, exam details, and important instructions for Bank of Baroda Peon Admit Card 2026.

Exam Date: 22 March 2026

Download Status: Active Now

How to Download Bank of Baroda Peon Admit Card 2026?

Follow these simple steps to download your Bank of Baroda Peon Admit Card 2026:

  1. Step 1: Visit the official Bank of Baroda website at bankofbaroda.in or click the direct download link provided above
  2. Step 2: Navigate to the "Careers" or "Current Openings" section on the homepage
  3. Step 3: Click on "Bank of Baroda Peon Mains Admit Card 2026" or relevant link for Office Assistant (Peon) Mains Admit Card
  4. Step 4: Enter your credentials:
    • Registration Number / Roll Number
    • Password or Date of Birth
  5. Step 5: Click on "Submit" or "Download Admit Card" button
  6. Step 6: Your Bank of Baroda Peon Admit Card 2026 will appear on screen
  7. Step 7: Download and save the PDF file on your device
  8. Step 8: Take 2-3 printouts for exam day (keep backup copies)

Pro Tip: Check all details on your admit card immediately after download. Report any discrepancies to the helpdesk (e.g., via http://cgrs.ibps.in) promptly.

Details on Bank of Baroda Peon Admit Card 2026

Your Bank of Baroda Peon Admit Card 2026 will contain the following information:

Candidate Information:

  • Candidate Name
  • Parent's Name
  • Date of Birth
  • Category
  • Gender
  • Registration/Roll Number
  • Candidate's Photograph and Signature

Exam Information:

  • Exam Name: Office Assistant (Peon) Mains Online Exam
  • Exam Date: 22 March 2026
  • Reporting Time
  • Exam Center Name and Complete Address
  • Exam Center Code

Important Instructions:

  • Documents to carry on exam day
  • Items allowed and not allowed in examination hall
  • Exam day instructions and rules

Documents Required on Bank of Baroda Peon Exam Day

Mandatory Documents (Originals):

  • Bank of Baroda Peon Admit Card 2026 (printed copy)
  • Valid Photo ID Proof (any one of the following):
    • Aadhaar Card
    • Voter ID Card
    • Driving License
    • PAN Card
    • Passport
  • Recent passport-size photographs (2 copies)

Not Allowed in Examination Hall:

  • Mobile phones and smartphones
  • Smartwatches and digital watches
  • Calculators and electronic devices
  • Books, notes, and study materials
  • Bags and pouches
  • Bluetooth devices and earphones

Important Instructions for Bank of Baroda Peon Exam 2026

Before Exam Day:

  • Download admit card well in advance
  • Verify exam center location on Google Maps
  • Plan your route and transportation
  • Arrange all required documents in advance
  • Keep backup copies of admit card
  • Read exam instructions carefully

On Exam Day:

  • Reach exam center 30-45 minutes before reporting time
  • Entry closes before exam starts (No late entry allowed)
  • Follow all guidelines and invigilator instructions
  • Keep calm and focused

What If Bank of Baroda Peon Admit Card Not Available?

If you're unable to download your Bank of Baroda Peon Admit Card 2026, try these solutions:

  1. Solution 1: Clear browser cache and cookies, then try again
  2. Solution 2: Try different web browser (Chrome, Firefox, etc.)
  3. Solution 3: Check your application status on the official website
  4. Solution 4: Contact helpdesk (e.g., http://cgrs.ibps.in) immediately

Important: Act immediately if facing issues downloading admit card. Don’t wait until exam day. Contact support at least 2 days before the exam.

Stay Updated: Bookmark for latest updates on Bank of Baroda Peon Result 2026, answer key, and all recruitment notifications.

Name of the Post: CRPF Constable Tradesman Online Form 2026

Post Date: 09-04-2026

Total Vacancy: 9195 Posts

Brief Information: The Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) has announced a notification for the recruitment of Constable Tradesman Vacancy. Eligible Candidates who are interested in the vacancy details & completed all eligibility criteria can read the notification & Apply.

CRPF Recruitment 2026 Notification Overview

The Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) has officially released a recruitment notification for Constable Tradesman . For all details regarding the recruitment process, eligibility, and application procedure, refer to the official notification. Eligible candidates can download it from the link below.

Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF)

Constable Tradesman Vacancy 2026

WWW.OnlyJobs

Application Fee

  • ForGeneral, OBC, EWS :Rs. 100/-Answer Key Access
  • Exempted for SC, ST, Female (all categories) and Ex-servicemen candidates

CRPF Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

  • Starting Date for Apply Online: 20-04-2026
  • Last Date for Apply Online: 19-05-2026

CRPF Recruitment 2026 Age Limit (as on 01-01-2026)

  • Age Limit for Constable (Driver): 21-27 Years
  • Constable (Motor Mechanic Vehicle / Cobbler / Carpenter / Tailor / Brass Band / Pipe Band / Gardner / Bugler / Painter / Cook / Barber cum Hair Dresser / Washer Man & Women / Water Carrier / Safai Karamchari / Mason / Plumber / Electrician): 18-23 Years
  • Age relaxation is applicable as per rules.

Qualification

  • Driver: Matriculation + Heavy Transport Vehicle Driving License
  • Motor Mechanic Vehicle: Matriculation + 2 years ITI in Mechanic Motor Vehicle + 1 year experience
  • All other Tradesmen: Matriculation + proficient in respective trade
  • Pioneer Wing (Mason/Electrician): Matriculation + 1 year experience in trade (ITI preferred)

Pay Scale

  • Pay Level-3: ₹21,700 - 69,100

Selection Process

The recruitment process will consist of Physical Efficiency Test (PET), Physical Standards Test (PST), Computer Based Test (CBT), Trade/Skill Test, Document Verification (DV) and Medical Examination (DME/RME).

  • Physical Efficiency Test (PET): Qualifying in nature.
    • For Constable (Driver, Motor Mechanic Vehicle, Gardner, Painter, Carpenter, Brass Band, Pipe Band, Cobbler, Tailor & Bugler): Male – 5 Kms race in 24 minutes; Female – 1.6 Kms in 8.30 minutes.
    • For Constable (Cook/Water Carrier, Barber & Hair Dresser, Washer man & Washer women, Safai Karmachari): Male – 1.6 Kms in 10 minutes; Female – 1.6 Kms in 12 minutes.
    • For Pioneer Wing (Mason / Electrician): Male – 1.6 Kms in 09 minutes.
    • Pregnant women candidates will be declared temporarily unfit.
  • Physical Standards Test (PST): Height, Chest (for males only) & Weight (proportionate to height & age).
    • Height: Male – 170 Cms (minimum); Female – 157 Cms (minimum). Relaxations applicable for certain categories (ST, NE States, Gorkhas, etc.).
    • Chest (Male only): Un-expanded – 80 Cms, Minimum expansion – 05 Cms. Relaxations applicable.
    • Candidates failing PST will be eliminated.
  • Computer Based Test (CBT): 100 marks (100 MCQs), duration 02 hours.
    • General Intelligence & Reasoning – 25 Qs / 25 Marks
    • General Knowledge & General Awareness – 25 Qs / 25 Marks
    • Elementary Mathematics – 25 Qs / 25 Marks
    • English/Hindi – 25 Qs / 25 Marks
    • Negative marking of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer.
    • Qualifying marks: UR – 30%, EWS/OBC – 25%, SC/ST & others – 20%.
    • Marks will be normalized. Merit list prepared on CBT marks.
  • Trade/Skill Test: Qualifying in nature (50 marks, minimum 20 marks required). Practical assessment of the applied trade/post.
  • Document Verification (DV): Original documents (Matric certificate, Domicile/PRC, Trade certificates, Caste/EWS/ESM certificates, etc.) will be verified.
  • Detailed Medical Examination (DME) / Review Medical Examination (RME): As per Uniform Guidelines issued by MHA. Temporary unfitness allowed only for pregnancy. Final selection subject to medical fitness.
  • Final merit list prepared on the basis of marks obtained in CBT (subject to qualifying all stages). Separate merit lists for male/female and each State/UT (All India basis for Pioneer posts).

How to Apply

Application Mode: Online only through CRPF website. No other mode will be accepted.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Visit the official website www.rect.crpf.gov.in.
  2. Click on the link for “Recruitment for the post of Constable (Technical & Tradesmen and Pioneer) 2026”.
  3. Register by providing basic details and generate Provisional Registration Number & Password.
  4. Fill the online application form carefully (personal details, educational qualification, trade/post applied, State/UT of domicile, etc.).
  5. Upload scanned copies of recent colour passport size photograph, signature, left thumb impression and handwritten declaration as per specifications.
  6. Pay the application fee online (if applicable) through BHIM, UPI, Net Banking, Debit/Credit Card or CSC Wallet.
  7. Submit the application and take printout of the application form & e-receipt for future reference.
  8. Candidates should bring two coloured printouts of the Admit Card at every stage of examination.

Application Fee:

  • ₹100/- (for male candidates of General, EWS & OBC only)
  • Exempted for SC, ST, Female (all categories) & Ex-servicemen candidates.

Note: Applications received without prescribed fee (where applicable) shall be summarily rejected. No hard copy of application is required to be sent.

General Instructions

  • Recruitment is domicile-based for Technical & Tradesmen posts and All India basis for Pioneer posts. Candidates must submit valid Domicile/Permanent Residence Certificate of the applied State/UT at DV/DME.
  • Candidates must be citizens of India.
  • Disqualification: Candidates who have entered into or contracted marriage with a person having a spouse living or who, having a spouse living, have entered into or contracted marriage with any person (subject to personal law exemptions).
  • Age & educational qualification will be reckoned as on 01-01-2026.
  • Only Indian Nationals need to apply. Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification.
  • No TA/DA will be paid for appearing in PET/PST/CBT/Trade Test/DV/DME.
  • Mere submission of application or issue of Admit Card does not confer any right to selection.
  • The CRPF reserves the right to cancel/restrict/enlarge/modify the recruitment process without assigning any reason.
  • Any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be subject to the jurisdiction of Courts having jurisdiction over the Examination Centres.
  • All correspondence will be made only through the registered email ID/mobile number provided by the candidate.
  • Candidates are advised to visit www.rect.crpf.gov.in regularly for updates.
  • For any queries, candidates may contact CRPF Helpline No. 011-26160255.

Selection Process

Selection will be made through shortlisting of applications followed by Interview (or Online Examination + Interview, if conducted by the Bank).

  • Candidates fulfilling the eligibility criteria will be shortlisted for Interview (or Online Test + Interview) based on the information furnished in the online application and supporting documents.
  • The Bank reserves the right to conduct Online Examination / Group Discussion / Interview or any combination thereof as per its requirement.
  • Final selection will be based on the candidate’s performance in the selection process, subject to verification of documents and medical fitness.
  • The Bank may shortlist candidates in the ratio decided by the Bank and call them for Interview.
  • Merit list will be prepared post-Interview and provisional offer of appointment will be issued to the selected candidates.
  • The decision of the Bank in all matters relating to selection shall be final and binding.

How to Apply

Application Mode: Online only through the official website of Indian Bank. No other mode of application will be accepted.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Visit the official website of Indian Bank and go to the “Recruitment” / “Careers” section.
  2. Click on the link for “Recruitment of Specialist Officers - 2026” and register using a valid email ID and mobile number.
  3. Fill the online application form carefully with all mandatory details (personal information, educational qualification, experience, category etc.).
  4. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature and all required documents/certificates (educational, experience, category, PwBD etc.) as per specifications.
  5. Pay the requisite application fee / intimation charges online.
  6. Submit the application and take a printout of the final submitted application form for future reference.
  7. A candidate can apply for only one post. In case of multiple applications, only the latest valid (completed) application will be retained.

General Instructions

  • Cut-off date for determining eligibility (age, qualification, experience, etc.) is 01-04-2026.
  • Only one application should be submitted by any candidate. Multiple applications will lead to cancellation of candidature.
  • Candidates must ensure that they fulfil all the eligibility criteria mentioned in the advertisement. Mere submission of application does not guarantee eligibility.
  • The number of vacancies is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the Bank.
  • Reservation for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD will be provided as per Government of India guidelines. PwBD reservation is on horizontal basis.
  • Candidates belonging to reserved categories must upload valid category certificates issued on or before the last date of registration.
  • All educational qualifications and experience must be from Government recognized Universities / Institutions and should be active as on the date of application.
  • The Bank reserves the right to cancel / modify the recruitment process at any stage without assigning any reason.
  • Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification of the candidature.
  • Decision of the Bank in all matters relating to eligibility, acceptance or rejection of applications, mode of selection, etc. shall be final and binding on the candidates.
  • All future communications (call letters, results, etc.) will be sent only through the registered email ID and SMS. Candidates are advised to keep the same email ID and mobile number active till completion of the recruitment process.
  • Any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be subject to the jurisdiction of Courts in Chennai only

Other Terms and Conditions

How to Apply for CSC e‑Governance Services Indian Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator Recruitment 2026

Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online only through the official CSC e‑Governance Services Indian website. Follow the steps given below to submit your application form.

Visit Official Website

  • Go to the official CSC e‑Governance Services Indian website: csc.gov.in.

  • On the homepage, navigate to the Recruitment/Careers/Latest Opening section.

Open Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator Application Link

  • Find and click on the notification/link titled “CSC e‑Governance Services Indian Aadhaar Supervisor/ Operator Recruitment 2026” or similar.

  • Click on the Apply Online button to proceed to the application page.

Registration (New Users)

  • Click on New Registration (if applying for the first time).

  • Enter basic details such as Name, Mobile Number, Email ID, Date of Birth, and create a password.

  • Submit the details to generate your Registration ID and Password (note these for future login).

Login and Fill Application Form

  • Login using your Registration ID and Password.

  • Fill in the online application form carefully with personal details, contact details, educational qualification, Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator certificate details, and preferred state/district.

  • Ensure that all details match your certificates and Aadhaar data.

Upload Documents

  • Upload scanned copies of the following documents in the prescribed format and size (as mentioned in the notification):

    • Recent passport size photograph

    • Signature

    • 10th/12th/ITI/Polytechnic Diploma certificates and mark sheets

    • Aadhaar Operator/Supervisor Certificate issued by UIDAI‑authorized Testing & Certifying Agency

    • Any other documents required as per notification

  • Preview the uploaded documents and confirm that they are clear and readable.

Application Fee Payment (If Applicable)

  • Check the Application Fee section in the official notification.

  • If any fee is prescribed, pay it online through available modes such as Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking, or UPI.

  • After successful payment, download/print the fee receipt.

Final Submission

  • Carefully review all the details filled in the application form in the Preview section.

  • If required, make corrections before final submission.

  • Click on Final Submit to complete the application process.

Print Application Form

  • After successful submission, download and take a printout of the final submitted application form for future reference.

  • Keep the Registration ID, Password, and printout safe for further recruitment stages.

Selection Process

The direct recruitment will be done through a three-stage process:

  • Stage 1: Screening Test (qualifying in nature)
    • Online MCQ (150 questions, 3 hours duration) on Reasoning, Basic Mathematics, English, Aptitude, Attitude and Leadership Quality.
    • Medium: English only.
    • Qualifying marks: Minimum 60% (relaxable to 50% for OBC and 40% for SC/ST/PwBD if sufficient candidates not available).
    • Shortlisting ratio: Restricted to 1:6 (six candidates per post) in order of merit in each category.
  • Stage 2: Evaluation of Academic Records
    • Applications qualifying the Screening Test will be evaluated by a Screening Evaluation Committee.
    • Marks awarded as per detailed norms given in Annexure-I (Scientist-C) and Annexure-II (Scientist-D) (total 120 marks).
    • Shortlisting ratio for Interview: 1:3 (three candidates per post) in order of merit.
  • Stage 3: Personal Interaction / Interview
    • 30 marks.
    • Conducted at Delhi or any other location/mode approved by competent authority.
    • Final Merit List: Marks of Evaluation (120) + Interview (30) = 150 marks.

Document Verification: Original documents will be verified prior to the interview. Candidates failing to produce all required original documents will be debarred.

Note: Screening Test is qualifying only. Final selection is based on combined marks of Evaluation + Interview. Normalization of marks will be applied if the test is conducted in multiple batches.

How to Apply

Candidates must apply ONLINE only at https://recruitment.nic.in/ between 08-04-2026 to 30-04-2026 (05:30 PM). No other mode of application will be accepted.

Four Mandatory Steps:

  1. Registration
  2. Fill Online Application Form & Upload Documents (Photo, Signature, Age Proof, Qualification Certificates, Experience Certificates, Caste/PwBD Certificates, NOC/CCA Declaration, SOP, etc. – full checklist given in the advertisement)
  3. Payment of Application Fee (if applicable)
  4. Download Confirmation Page

Important:

  • Application is complete only after all four steps.
  • Aadhaar-based authentication will be used at every stage.
  • Candidates can apply for more than one post but must pay separate fee for each.
  • Change of category is not permitted after submission.
  • Serving Government employees must upload signed CCA Declaration & Acknowledgement (Annexure-VI).

Application Fee (non-refundable):

  • Nil – SC/ST/PwBD/Women candidates
  • Rs. 800/- per post – General and all others

General Instructions

  • The number of posts is tentative and may increase or decrease.
  • Place of posting: Anywhere in India and outside India.
  • Cut-off date for age, qualification and experience: 21-11-2022.
  • All communications will be sent only through registered email ID and mobile number. Candidates must regularly check their email/SMS and the official recruitment portal.
  • No TA/DA will be paid for attending the Screening Test or Interview.
  • Mere fulfilling of minimum eligibility does not entitle a candidate to be called for the next stage.
  • Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification.
  • Any false information or forged documents will result in cancellation of candidature and legal action.
  • Decision of NIC/MeitY in all matters relating to recruitment shall be final.
  • Jurisdiction: Any dispute will be subject to the jurisdiction of courts in Delhi only.

Selection Process

Selection will be made through Computer Based Test (CBT) only.

  • The CBT will comprise of objective type questions with multiple choice of answers. The Question Paper and instructions will be Bilingual (English/Hindi). However, in case of any error in Hindi version, the English version of the question shall be valid & final.
  • The CBT will consist of two sections (Section – ‘A’ & Section – ‘B’):
    • Section – ‘A’: Questions related to Technical Knowledge (Discipline related) with 80 Multiple Choice Questions (MCQ).
    • Section – ‘B’: Questions related to General Awareness/Aptitude and Knowledge of CIL/SECL with 20 multiple choice questions (MCQ).
  • Each question will carry 1 mark and there is no penalty for wrong answer. No marks will be awarded for un-attempted questions.
  • Syllabus for the Computer Based Test (CBT) is attached as Annexure-A in the notification.
  • Selection of candidates in merit panel will be subject to securing of cut-off marks (Minimum Qualifying marks) in the Computer Based Test for concerned post with respect to their category as mentioned below and fulfilling prescribed eligibility criteria:
Category Qualifying marks in CBT
For General/EWS Candidates 45 marks
For OBC(NCL)/ESM Candidates 40 marks
For SC/ST Candidates 35 marks
  • Result of the Computer Based Test (CBT) will be published on SECL website as early as possible after successful completion of the CBT.
  • Provisional merit panels of candidates who secure the requisite cut-off marks and above and fulfilling the eligibility criteria will be published, in the order of marks scored, after resolving tie.
  • In case of tie of marks between 02 or more candidates, the merit-list will be prepared as per the following criteria: a) Date of Birth (As per the descending order of age of candidates) b) Seniority as per the Date of issue of Statutory Certificate c) In case still tied up, alphabetical order of the name of the candidates as recorded in the application form may be taken into consideration. d) In case still tied-up, the Selection Committee will decide the criteria for deciding the merit position of tied candidates.
  • Final merit panel will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the Computer Based Test.
  • Such candidates who would score equal or above the cut off (Minimum qualifying marks as provided in Table D) and as per vacancy available in each category, would only be notified for appearing for scrutiny/ verification of their testimonials supporting their claim of candidature, in order of their respective merit position drawn on the basis of marks scored in Computer Based Test (CBT) for different positions up to the extent the positions remain unfilled. The candidates shall have to appear along with all original certificates/ documents as per eligibility criteria notified in this Employment Notification for scrutiny/ verification. The date, time and venue for scrutiny/ verification shall be notified on SECL website and the candidates shall have to report accordingly. In the event of failure to report with all original certificates and testimonials within the stipulated time, the candidature of such candidate should stand cancelled.
  • Provisional Appointment Letter will be issued to the successful candidates in accordance with their relative merit position and with due consideration of the reservation of posts to the reserved category candidates and against vacancies.
  • If it is found during the Scrutiny/ Verification, or during the course of training, or even during the course of employment, that the candidate is not fulfilling the eligibility criteria in any respect, suppressing of facts or false declaration pertaining to his Employment/Caste/ Testimonials etc. then his/her candidature/ employment will be cancelled/terminated.

Note: In case, a candidate, selected for a particular post & declared as UNFIT during Initial Medical Examination, the case would be referred to Apex Medical Board of the Company within one month based on the written/physical representation and the decision of the Apex Medical Board will be final. If the Candidate is declared UNFIT by the Apex Medical Board, his/ her selection shall stand automatically cancelled. Such candidates will be communicated the decision of the Apex Medical Board as well as regarding cancellation of their selection.

2. How to Apply

Application Mode: Online only through SECL website. No other mode (post, email, offline) will be accepted.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Visit the official website of SECL https://secl-cil.in/ → Career → Employment Notification for Direct Recruitment of various Statutory posts → Apply online.
  2. Employment Notification is available in both English and Hindi. Candidates may select either of them at their convenience.
  3. While applying online, candidate needs to upload the scanned copies of:
    • Recent passport size colour photograph (not older than 3 weeks in jpg/jpeg format)
    • Signature with Black ink pen (in jpg/jpeg format)
    • Matriculation certificate or equivalent examination certificate or Degree or Diploma Certificate for the post of Mining Sirdar & Dy. Surveyor (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • Matriculate or equivalent examination passed from any recognized Board of examination & Diploma or relevant and higher qualification (Minimum 3 years course) in Electrical Engineering from any Institute recognized by the Government, for the post of Assistant Foreman (Electrical) (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • Valid Caste Certificate OBC (NCL)/SC/ST candidates and Income Certificate for EWS Candidates (if applicable) (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • Ex-Servicemen Certificates (if applicable) (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • PwBD Certificates (if applicable) (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • Competency certificate of Mining Sirdar/Surveyor (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • Gas Testing & First Aid Certificate (for Mining Sirdar only) (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
  4. Register by filling up necessary details (Post opted, name, Mobile No. and e-mail ID and click on Submit Button).
  5. Check the User ID and password received on your e-mail and mobile number after successful registration.
  6. Re-login to your account entering the User ID and Password received on Mobile No. and e-mail ID.
  7. Click on the option to go to application form and fill all the necessary details in the respective fields and upload all the required documents including Photograph and Signature.
  8. Check Preview of the Application Form and make corrections, if any.
  9. Press Submit Button and make Online Payment of the fees through Debit Card/Credit Card/Net Banking (as applicable).
  10. Keep the print out of the online application form filled for future references.

Application Fee:

Category Amount (₹)
Unreserved (UR) /OBC- (Creamy Layer & Non-Creamy Layer) / EWS ₹1000/- + GST ₹180/- = ₹1180/-
SC/ ST/ESM/PwBD/ Departmental Candidates/Female Candidates Nil

Note: Applications without the prescribed fee will not be entertained and summarily rejected. No representation against such rejection would be entertained. Fee once paid shall neither be refunded nor be adjusted under any circumstances. Any tax or Bank charges other than the application fees will be borne by the candidates. Application fee will be paid through online mode only.

General Instructions

  • Application should be submitted through ONLINE Mode only.
  • All qualifications should be recognized by AICTE / UGC / appropriate Indian Statutory Authorities.
  • Before submitting the application, the candidate must ensure that he/ she fulfills all the eligibility criteria and other norms mentioned in the Employment Notification. He/ she may cross check the information such as Date of Birth, Category, Contact Details, and Qualifications etc. furnished in the application form before finally submitting the same online as no correction would be possible later and no correspondence will be considered in this regard.
  • The decision of SECL management regarding eligibility criteria, acceptance or rejection of applications, penalty for false information, mode of selection, selection and posting of selected candidates, will be final and binding on the candidates and no enquiry/correspondence will be entertained in this regard.
  • Mere issuance of Admit Card/ Call Letter to a candidate will not imply that his/ her candidature has been finally cleared by SECL. The candidature shall remain provisional till such time SECL verifies the eligibility conditions with reference to original documents after the candidate qualifies in the CBT and confirms appointment.
  • Candidates should ensure that they fulfill the eligibility criteria prescribed for the post for which they have applied. In case it is found at any stage of the selection process or even after the appointment that the candidate has submitted false or incorrect information or suppressed any relevant information/ material facts or does not fulfill the eligibility criteria, his/her candidature/ service is liable for rejection/ termination without notice.
  • No relaxation in the minimum educational qualification, as prescribed in Clause - '2' of this Employment Notification, is admissible for any category of candidate.
  • Wherever Grade (CGPA/ OGP/ DGPA etc.) is awarded, a certificate duly issued by concerned Board/University for conversion of Grade to percentage is to be produced by the candidate at the time of Scrutiny/ Verification of Original documents.
  • Age and all other eligibility criteria shall be reckoned as on the cut-off date as prescribed under this Employment Notification.
  • Candidates should carefully fill up their category (General/ SC/ ST/ OBC- Non Creamy Layer/ Ex-Servicemen/ EWS/PwBD etc.) in the Online Application Form. No change of category shall be allowed at the later stage of selection process.
  • Ex-servicemen must produce discharge certificate issued by Army/Navy/ Air Force of Indian Union at the time of document verification.
  • All documents (Caste Certificate, Educational Qualifications, EWS certificate, etc.) as per applicability and as provided by the candidate will be verified from the office of issuing authority and candidature will be cancelled if the certificates are not found satisfactory/ genuine at any subsequent stage of Computer Based Test (CBT).
  • The candidates must have an active E-mail Id & Mobile number which must remain valid for at least next one year. All correspondences with the candidates shall be done either through Speed Post/ Registered e-mail id & Mobile number (as may be necessary) and all general information shall be provided through SECL website. However, admit card will be provided only through SECL website and the candidates will have to download the admit card. The responsibilities of receiving, downloading and printing of application form, admit card and other information shall be of candidates. SECL will not be responsible for any loss of document due to invalid/ wrong/ incomplete contact details provided by the candidate or for delay/ non-receipt of information. In case of any problems faced by the candidates in filling up the online application they may refer the "Help Desk Option" available in the individual login portal. For queries other than the Online Application Form, candidates may write to Email Id: [email protected] only or can call us on 9425598240.
  • Candidates are advised to apply much before the closing date for submission of Online Application mentioned in this Employment Notification to avoid last minute network congestion.
  • SECL will not take any responsibility for the candidates not being able to submit their applications Online within the prescribed closing dates on account of any reasons beyond the control of SECL.
  • SECL reserves the right to shortlist candidates for Computer Based Test, reschedule the test date & venue etc. or alter any of the advertised condition depending upon the circumstances.
  • SECL will not bear any liability on account of service bond/ salary/ leave salary/ pension contribution, etc, if any, of previous employment of any candidate already working in Central Govt./ State Govt./ Autonomous Body/ Public Sector Undertaking.
  • Candidates employed in Govt./ Semi Govt./ Public Sector Undertaking (including departmental candidates) / Autonomous Bodies are required to produce 'No Objection Certificate' at the time of Scrutiny /Verification of original documents if shortlisted.
  • Selected candidates could be posted in any of the Mines/Establishments of SECL and are liable to be transferred to other subsidiaries of Coal India Limited any time during their service as may be required.
  • Appointment of selected candidates will be subject to being found medically fit in the Company's Medical Examination as per the laid down Rules related to Medical Examination of CIL.
  • Selection of candidate shall be provisional, subject to verification of documents relating to eligibility criteria, character, antecedents and other documents submitted by the candidate and is also subject to his/ her meeting other requirements applicable for appointment under the rules of SECL.
  • Appointment of the candidate in SECL will be subject to verification of Character & Antecedents by the prescribed authorities and if any discrepancy / suppression is found, the candidature of the candidate will be cancelled and decision of SECL in this regard shall be final.
  • The date of Computer Based Test (CBT) and other details will be subsequently informed. Candidates will have to appear at the Centre/ Venue allotted to them by SECL for the Computer Based Test (CBT). No change in Centre/ Venue is permissible and no application shall be entertained in this regard.
  • SC/ ST candidates called for Computer Based Test (CBT) will be paid up to IInd Class Non-AC fare as per rules. However they have to submit their claims in duly filled appropriate TA Claim format provided at the examination centre along with all desired enclosures like caste certificate, photocopy of Onward (mandatory) and return Journey (optional) of railway ticket / bus receipt ticket to the room invigilator after attempting Computer Based Test (CBT) only, subsequent claims as well as claims with incomplete information will not be entertained. The distance of examination centre will be calculated from the Permanent address of the candidate or from place of actual journey whichever is shorter. In case, candidate has submitted only onward journey ticket and not submitted return journey tickets then the claims will be considered up to the double of onward journey fare and that will be paid as per rule.
  • No TA/DA will be paid for appearing in the CBT, scrutiny/ verification of certificates and documents or for joining on being selected except otherwise as provided in this Employment Notification.
  • Candidate will appear for the CBT at the exam center at his/her owns risk and South Eastern Coalfields Limited will not be responsible for any injury or losses etc.
  • SECL management reserves the right to modify/ alter/ restrict/ enlarge/ cancel the recruitment process, and revise the number of vacancies, at its discretion without assigning any reasons whatsoever. The decision of the SECL management will be final and no appeal will be entertained in this regard.
  • All future communication/ information regarding this recruitment will be made available on SECL website. Candidates are advised to check/ visit SECL website regularly for further updates.
  • Court of Jurisdiction for any dispute will be Honorable High Court of Chhattisgarh, Bilaspur.
  • In case of any ambiguity/ confusion, English Version of Employment Notification will be considered valid.
  • Canvassing in any form will be treated as dis-qualification.
  • At the time of Computer Based Test (CBT), if a candidate is found guilty of using unfair means or impersonating or misbehaving in the examination hall or resorting to any other unfair means in connection with his/her candidature for the selection; or obtaining support of his/ her candidature by any means, such candidates will be liable to criminal prosecution and will be disqualified from the Computer Based Test (CBT). The Company may also debar such candidates from participating in any selection process in the Company in future.
  • If it is detected that any candidate has furnished any false information / particulars with regard to his/ her qualification, Category/caste and experience in the Application Form or in any document and/ or misrepresented / suppressed any material fact in the application form, for securing appointment, his/ her service will be terminated forthwith during probation period or even after confirmation of the service.
  • The Candidates whose postings are finalized after CBT, Verification and IME will be disqualified if they put external pressure for place of posting.

Central Bank of India Recruitment 2026 Notification Overview

The Central Bank of India has officially released a recruitment notification for Specialist Officers. For all details regarding the recruitment process, eligibility, and application procedure, refer to the official notification. Eligible candidates can download it from the link below.

Qualification

Preferred: MBA/PGDM in Finance, FRM/CFA/CQF certifications.
  • Asset Liability Management (Scale III/II): BE/B.Tech in IT/CS/Software Engg. or MBA/CA + 4/3 years total experience (at least 3/2 years in Risk Management in Banks/NBFCs).
  • Digital Risk Management (Scale III/II): Bachelor’s in Computer Science/IT/Cybersecurity + 4/3 years total experience (at least 3/2 years in Risk Management). Preferred: CISA/CISM/CRISC/CISSP.
  • Enterprise Risk (Scale III): BE/B.Tech in IT/CS/Software Engg. or MBA/CA + 5 years total experience (at least 3 years in Risk Management).
  • Market Risk (Scale III/II): BE/B.Tech in IT/CS/Software Engg. or MBA/CA + 4/3 years total experience (at least 3/2 years in Risk Management).

Selection Process

  • The selection process for Central Bank of India Specialist Officers in Risk Management streams consists of an online written examination followed by a personal interview.

  • The final merit will be prepared by giving 70% weightage to the online written test and 30% weightage to the interview, subject to document verification and medical fitness.

  • Only those candidates who qualify the online exam and meet the bank’s cutoff will be called for the interview in a prescribed ratio to the number of vacancies.

How to Apply

Visit official website

  • Go to the official Central Bank of India website: centralbank.bank.in.

  • Open the “Recruitment/ Careers” section and click on the link for “Recruitment Process for Selection of Officers in Specialist Category in Risk Management Streams.”

Open Apply Online link

  • On the recruitment page, click on “Click Here to Apply” / “Apply Online” which redirects to the online registration page (IBPS application link for CBI SO).

  • Read the detailed notification, “How to Apply” and FAQ on the registration page carefully before proceeding.

New registration

  • Click on “New Registration” and enter basic details (name, contact number, email ID, etc.) to generate a Registration Number and Password.

  • Note down the Registration Number and Password for future login and form completion.

Fill online application form

  • Log in using Registration Number and Password and select the relevant post/scale (Analytics & Model Risk, Asset Liability Management, Digital Risk Management, Enterprise Risk, Market Risk in Scale IV/III/II as applicable).

  • Fill personal details, category, domicile, address, qualification (B.Sc/B.Tech/BE/MBA/PGDM etc.) and post-qualification experience in Risk/IT/ALM/Digital Risk as per eligibility.

  • Ensure all experience details (years, organisation, role) match what is required for the chosen scale and stream.

Upload required documents

  • Upload recent passport-size photograph, signature, left thumb impression and handwritten declaration in the prescribed dimensions and file size, as per the instructions in the notification.

  • Upload supporting documents (if asked at application stage) like educational certificates, experience certificates and category certificates in the specified format.

Pay application fee

  • Pay the application fee online: ₹175/- (intimation charges) for SC/ST/PwBD/Women candidates and ₹850/- (application fee + intimation charges) for all other candidates, through debit card/credit card/net banking/UPI.

  • After successful payment, verify that the fee status shows as “paid” and proceed to final submit. Applications without successful fee payment (where applicable) will be treated as incomplete.

Final submission and print

  • Review all details carefully; edit if required before final submission, as no changes are allowed after final submit.

  • Click on “Final Submit” and generate your application form.

  • Download and take a printout of the e-receipt and submitted application form for future reference, online exam and interview/document verification stages.

General Instructions for Candidates

  • Apply online only; no other mode of application will be accepted.

  • Ensure you fall within the required age limits for Scale IV (20–45 years) and Scale III/II (20–35 years) with applicable relaxations as per Government/Bank rules.

  • Enter details exactly as per your certificates; any mismatch or false information may lead to rejection at any stage of the recruitment process.

  • Keep the registered mobile number and email ID active; all communication regarding exam date, admit card, interview schedule, etc., will be sent on these only.

  • Candidates are advised to complete the application process well before 30-04-2026 to avoid last-minute technical issues.

Selection Process

Selection will be made through Shortlisting of candidates followed by Interview.

  • Applications will be scrutinized as per eligibility criteria mentioned in the advertisement.
  • Shortlisted candidates will be called for interview (date, time and venue will be informed separately).
  • Candidates have to bear their own travelling cost for attending the interview.
  • Self-attested copies of educational qualifications and experience certificates will be submitted at the time of interview. Original documents will be required for verification.
  • Final selection will be based on performance in the interview and verification of documents.
  • NPS Trust reserves the right to modify the selection procedure, if deemed fit.
  • Mere fulfilling of eligibility criteria does not entitle a candidate to be called for interview.
  • The decision of NPS Trust in all matters relating to eligibility, shortlisting and selection shall be final and binding.
  • Appointment is purely contractual for 3 years (extendable by 2 years subject to outstanding performance). No claim for regular / permanent appointment.

How to Apply

Application Mode: By Email only. No other mode (post, online portal, hand delivery etc.) will be accepted.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Download and fill the prescribed Application Format (Section II of the notification) in legible PDF / type-written format.
  2. Paste recent passport-size photograph and sign across it.
  3. Attach self-attested copies of all required documents (DOB proof, educational certificates & mark sheets, experience certificates, relieving letters, caste / category certificate if applicable, Annexure-A for OBC if required, CV / Resume etc.).
  4. Send the complete application along with supporting documents by email to [email protected].
  5. Mention the exact subject line: “Recruitment of Staff (<Name of the Post & Department>) on Contract Basis”.
  6. Candidates applying for multiple posts must send separate applications for each post.
  7. Applications received after the due date / time or incomplete applications will be summarily rejected.

Note: Only one application per post will be considered (latest application in case of multiple submissions).

General Instructions

  • Cut-off date for determining eligibility (age, qualification, experience) is 28-02-2026.
  • Appointment is purely contractual in nature. Selected candidates shall have no right for permanent absorption in NPS Trust under any circumstances.
  • Initial contract period is 3 years, extendable by 2 years (one year at a time) subject to satisfactory performance.
  • Performance will be reviewed half-yearly. Unsatisfactory performance may lead to termination of contract.
  • Termination of contract can be done by either party by giving 2 months’ notice or payment in lieu thereof.
  • Place of posting is New Delhi (may be transferred anywhere in India as per requirement).
  • No residential accommodation will be provided.
  • Total yearly leave of 30 days (proportionate basis) with conditions mentioned in the notification.
  • Remuneration is fixed monthly gross salary with provision for annual increment based on performance.
  • Proficiency in English and Hindi (reading, writing & speaking) is essential.
  • Candidates working in Government / PSU must submit proper NOC / relieving letter at the time of joining.
  • Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification.
  • Corrigendum / Addendum, if any, will be published only on the official website www.npstrust.org.in.
  • Any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of Courts at New Delhi only.
  • NPS Trust reserves the right to cancel / modify the recruitment process at any stage without assigning any reason.

How to Apply for Sarkari Job Online 2026

  1. Find the right Sarkari Job: Use the vacancy table or sector links above on OnlyJobs. Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, or Engineering. Check the last date and eligibility carefully.

  2. Read the official notification: Click on the job listing to open the official notification PDF. Read all eligibility conditions — age limit, educational qualification, experience, category relaxations.

  3. Visit the official recruitment website: Never apply through third-party sites. Use the direct official link provided on OnlyJobs. Look for "Apply Online" or "Register" on the official portal.

  4. Register / Create account: Sign up using your valid email ID and active mobile number. Keep your Aadhaar, bank account details, and email accessible.

  5. Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualifications, category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS), address, and other required information accurately.

  6. Upload documents: Scan and upload recent passport-size photograph, signature, 10th certificate (for DOB proof), educational certificates, caste certificate (if applicable). Check file size and format (JPG/PDF as specified).

  7. Pay application fee: Pay via Net Banking, UPI, Debit/Credit Card as applicable. Some categories (SC/ST/PwD/Women) may be exempted from fee. Keep the payment receipt.

  8. Submit and save confirmation: Review all details before final submission. After submitting, download the application printout / PDF confirmation with your registration number. Store it safely for admit card, exam, and future reference.

Why Sarkari Job? Benefits of Government Employment in India

Job Security
Permanent employment — cannot be terminated without due process. Lifelong career stability.
7th Pay Commission Salary
Fixed salary + DA, HRA, TA allowances. DA revised every 6 months. Regular pay revisions.
Medical Benefits
Free or subsidised medical treatment for employee and family under CGHS / state health schemes.
Housing Benefits
Government quarters (where available) or HRA (8–27% of basic pay). No private rent worries.
Pension (NPS / OPS)
Post-retirement monthly pension under NPS or OPS (state-specific). Financial security after service.
Work-Life Balance
Fixed working hours. Paid casual, medical, earned leave. National and state holidays. No overtime culture.
Career Growth
Regular promotions through DPC, ACR-based upgrades, MACP benefits (3 financial upgrades guaranteed).
Children's Education Allowance
Reimbursement of children's school fees (₹2,812/month per child up to 2 children, revised periodically).

Frequently Asked Questions – Sarkari Job 2026

What is Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी)?

Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी) refers to government employment in India — jobs offered by central government departments, state governments, public sector undertakings (PSUs), banks, railways, defence forces, and other government-funded organisations. These jobs offer permanent employment, fixed salary as per 7th Pay Commission, pension, medical benefits, and unmatched job security.

How to find Sarkari Job vacancy 2026 online?

To find online Sarkari Job vacancies in 2026: (1) Visit OnlyJobs daily for updated notifications. (2) Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, PG. (3) Filter by sector — Railway, Banking, SSC, Defence, State PSC, Teaching. (4) Check last dates carefully. (5) Apply through the official department website using the direct link provided. Enable push notifications on OnlyJobs for instant alerts.

What Sarkari Job can I get after 10th pass?

After 10th pass, you can apply for: Railway Group D (RRB), Indian Army Soldier posts, Postal Mail Guard, SSC MTS / Havaldar, Constable GD, Anganwadi Worker/Helper, ITI Apprentice in PSUs (IOCL, HPCL, NTPC), MCL / Coal India posts, and various state government peon/chowkidar posts. Thousands of 10th pass Sarkari Job vacancies open every year.

What is the salary in Sarkari Job 2026?

Sarkari Job salary in 2026 follows the 7th Pay Commission: Group D / MTS: ₹22,000–₹26,000/month. Clerk / Group C: ₹32,000–₹48,000/month. Officer Grade (SSC CGL, Bank PO): ₹47,000–₹85,000/month. IAS/IPS (UPSC): ₹56,100–₹1,44,200 basic pay + DA, HRA, TA, medical and other allowances. State government salaries vary by state pay commission.

Which Bharat Sarkari Job has the most vacancies in 2026?

The largest Bharat Sarkari Job recruitments in 2026 include: Indian Army Agniveer (40,000+ posts), RRB Group D (22,000+ posts), SSC CGL (14,582 posts), SSC MTS (10,000+ posts), UP Police Constable (multiple), Bihar Police (multiple), IBPS Clerk (8,000+ posts), and various state PSC recruitments. Total active sarkari vacancies in India exceed 52,920 as of March 2026.

What is the best website to find Sarkari Job in India?

OnlyJobs is India's most trusted portal for Sarkari Job notifications since 2011, serving over 10 million users. Official direct sources include: ssc.nic.in (SSC), indianrailways.gov.in (Railways), upsc.gov.in (UPSC), ibps.in (Banking), and respective state PSC websites. OnlyJobs aggregates all official notifications in one place with direct apply links.

How to apply for Sarkari Job online in 2026?

To apply for Sarkari Job online: (1) Read official notification PDF. (2) Check eligibility. (3) Visit official recruitment website. (4) Register with email/mobile. (5) Fill application form. (6) Upload photo, signature, and certificates. (7) Pay fee via Net Banking/UPI/Debit card. (8) Submit and lication confirmation. Save the registration number for admit card and future reference.

What are the benefits of Sarkari Job in India?

Key benefits: (1) Lifelong job security. (2) 7th Pay Commission salary + DA revised biannually. (3) HRA (8–27% of basic). (4) Medical benefits (CGHS). (5) Pension under NPS/OPS. (6) Paid leave — casual, earned, medical. (7) Gratuity after 5 years. (8) Children's education allowance. (9) Fixed working hours and work-life balance. (10) Prestige and social recognition in Indian society.

What is the age limit for Sarkari Job?

Age limits vary by post: Group D (18–25 years), SSC CGL (18–32 years), Bank PO (20–30 years), UPSC Civil Services (21–32 years), Defence Agniveer (17.5–23 years). Age relaxations: SC/ST — 5 years, OBC — 3 years, PwD — 10 years, Ex-Servicemen — 3 years (general), Government servants — up to 5 years. Always verify in the official notification for each post.

What is Bharat Sarkari Job?

Bharat Sarkari Job means government job opportunities across the entire country of India (Bharat) — spanning central and state government vacancies across all 28 states and 8 union territories. The term emphasises pan-India coverage. OnlyJobs covers all Bharat Sarkari Job notifications from UPSC, SSC, Railways, Banking, Defence, state PSCs, and all other government departments.
