About OnlyJobs 2026

OnlyJobs (Government Jobs) are highly sought-after employment opportunities in India. These positions offer job security, good salary packages, allowances, and retirement benefits. Our platform provides comprehensive information about all types of government vacancies including:

  • Central Government Jobs: UPSC, SSC, Railway, Banking, Defence, PSUs
  • State Government Jobs: State PSCs, Police, Teachers, Healthcare workers
  • Qualification-based Jobs: 10th Pass, 12th Pass, Graduate, Post Graduate, Engineering
  • Category-wise Jobs: General, OBC, SC, ST, EWS, Ex-Servicemen, PwD

Frequently Asked Questions - OnlyJobs 2026

Q1: What is the eligibility for OnlyJobs?

Eligibility varies by position. Generally, candidates need to be Indian citizens aged 18-35 years (with age relaxation for reserved categories). Educational qualifications range from 10th pass to post-graduation depending on the job.

Q2: How to prepare for Government Job exams?

Preparation includes understanding the exam pattern, studying from standard books, practicing previous year papers, taking mock tests, and staying updated with current affairs. Focus on subjects like General Knowledge, Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, and English.

Q3: Which are the best Government Jobs in 2026?

Top government jobs include IAS/IPS (UPSC), Bank PO, SSC CGL, Railway Group A & B, Defence Services, PSU Jobs, State PCS, and Teaching positions. These offer excellent career growth, job security, and benefits.

Q4: What is the application process for OnlyJobs?

The process typically involves: 1) Online registration on the official website, 2) Filling application form, 3) Uploading documents (photo, signature, certificates), 4) Payment of application fee, 5) Downloading admit card, 6) Appearing for examination, 7) Document verification if selected.

Q5: How often are new OnlyJobs notifications released?

New government job notifications are released daily. Major recruitments like UPSC, SSC, IBPS have annual calendars, while state governments and PSUs release notifications throughout the year. We update our portal daily with 780+ active job listings.

Note: Honorarium figures are approximate and may vary. Refer to official state notification for exact figures. Additional benefits include ESI, provident fund (where applicable), and national health insurance.

Selection Process for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026

There is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Helper posts. Selection is merit-based on academic qualifications. For Supervisor and CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted.

How to Apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Online

  • Visit the official portal: Go to your state's official WCD / ICDS website or the direct link provided in the vacancy table above. For central information, visit wcd.nic.in.
  • Find the recruitment notification: Click on the "Recruitment" or "Anganwadi Recruitment 2026" section.
  • Download and read the notification: Download and read the official notification PDF carefully before applying.
  • Register / Login: Create a new account using your mobile number and Aadhaar. If already registered, log in with your credentials.
  • Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualification, address, and select your preferred district/ward. Ensure you apply only for a ward where you are a permanent resident.
  • Upload documents: Upload scanned copies of your passport-size photograph, signature, 10th/12th marksheet, Aadhaar card, and any caste/domicile certificate if required.
  • Review and submit: Check all entries carefully before final submission. Download and print the application confirmation / acknowledgement slip for your records.

Documents Required for Anganwadi Application

  • Aadhaar Card (Mandatory)
  • 10th Marksheet & Certificate
  • 12th Marksheet (for AWW)
  • Graduation Certificate (Supervisor)
  • Domicile / Residence Certificate
  • Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC)
  • Passport-size Photograph
  • Signature (scanned)
  • EWS Certificate (if applicable)
  • PwD Certificate (if applicable)

About this page: Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 information is compiled by the OnlyJobs editorial team directly from official state WCD / ICDS recruitment notifications. We verify all vacancy details, eligibility, and last dates before publishing. Page is updated daily as new notifications are released. For any discrepancy, always refer to the official notification PDF linked in the apply column above.

Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – FAQ's

How to apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 online?

Visit your state's official WCD / ICDS portal (link provided in table above). Register with mobile & Aadhaar, fill the application form, upload documents (photo, 10th certificate, Aadhaar, domicile), and submit before the last date. Print the application acknowledgement slip after submission.

What is the eligibility for Anganwadi Worker 2026?

Candidates must have passed 10th or 12th standard from a recognized board. Age limit is 18–35 years (varies by state). Must be a permanent resident of the ward/village for which vacancy exists. Only female candidates are eligible for Worker and Helper posts.

What is the salary of Anganwadi Worker in 2026?

Anganwadi Worker receives a monthly honorarium of ₹10,000–₹20,000 depending on the state (Karnataka and TN are highest at ~₹14,000). Anganwadi Helper gets ₹5,000–₹7,000/month. The Central Government pays ₹4,500/month as base honorarium, and states add their own component on top.

Is there a written exam for Anganwadi jobs?

No, there is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper posts. Selection is purely merit-based on academic qualification marks (10th / 12th percentage). For Supervisor / CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted depending on the state.

What is the age limit for Anganwadi Helper?

Age limit is 18 to 35 years as of 1st January 2026 in most states. SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (maximum age 40). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (maximum age 38). PwD candidates get additional relaxation as per state rules.

What documents are required for Anganwadi job application?

Required documents: Aadhaar card, 10th/12th marksheet and certificate, domicile/residence certificate, caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC), passport-size photograph, scanned signature, and EWS/PwD certificate if applicable. Graduation certificate is needed for Supervisor post applications.

What is ICDS recruitment 2026?

ICDS stands for Integrated Child Development Services - the government programme under which Anganwadi centres operate nationwide. ICDS recruitment 2026 refers to hiring of Anganwadi Workers, Helpers, Supervisors, and CDPOs by state WCD departments under the Ministry of Women & Child Development (wcd.nic.in).

Is Anganwadi Worker a permanent government job?

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers are honorary workers, not regular government employees. They receive a fixed monthly honorarium (not salary) and are entitled to benefits like ESI, health insurance, and maternity benefits. Supervisors and CDPOs are regular government employees in most states and receive full pay & grade pay.

Can I apply for Anganwadi job from a different district?

No. Anganwadi Workers and Helpers must be permanent residents of the same ward/village/district where the vacancy exists. A domicile or residence certificate is mandatory. Applications from outside the specified locality are rejected. For Supervisor/CDPO posts, the same district residency rule generally applies.

Forest Department Posts List

Forest departments work with a clear hierarchy from senior Indian Forest Service officers to field-level staff like Forest Guard and Forest Watcher. Understanding these posts helps candidates know growth opportunities and career progression in forest services.

  • Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (PCCF) / Head of Forest Force
  • Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (APCCF) / Additional Chief Conservator of Forests
  • Chief Conservator of Forests (CCF)
  • Conservator of Forests (CF)
  • Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF) / Divisional Forest Officer (DFO)
  • Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF)
  • Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Range Forest Officer (RFO)
  • Deputy Forest Ranger / Forest Inspector
  • Section Forest Officer / Forester / Van Daroga
  • Beat Forest Officer / Beat Guard / Forester Grade I
  • Forest Guard / Van Rakshak
  • Forest Watcher / Forest Worker / Plantation Worker
  • Forest Ranger (in states where used separately)
  • Field Guard

Most forest department recruitment notifications on this page focus on Forest Guard, Forester, Forest Range Officer, Field Guard and similar Group B, C and D posts.

Eligibility for Forest Department Jobs

Eligibility for forest department jobs depends on the post, state and recruitment authority. Below is a general idea of qualification and age for popular posts like Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer. Candidates should always check the official notification for detailed eligibility criteria.

Education Qualification for Forest Guard, Forester, Ranger

  • Forest Guard / Field Guard / Forest Watcher / Beat Guard: Usually 10th pass or 12th pass from a recognized board, knowledge of local language, and basic physical standards as per state rules.
  • Forester / Section Forest Officer / Deputy Forest Ranger: Generally 12th pass or graduate in any stream or science-related subjects, with physical standards and sometimes driving or technical skills depending on the post.
  • Forest Range Officer (RFO) / Forest Ranger / ACF: Normally graduate degree in Science, Agriculture, Forestry, Environmental Science, Engineering or related disciplines, with higher physical standards and sometimes a competitive exam similar to state PCS level.
  • IFS / Indian Forest Service and senior posts (DCF, CF, CCF, PCCF): Filled through UPSC Indian Forest Service Examination and promotions, requiring a graduate degree and all-India competitive selection.

Age Limit & Relaxation

  • Forest Guard / Forester / Field Guard: Minimum age is usually 18 years and maximum age is around 27–30 years, with age relaxation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, Ex-servicemen and other categories as per government rules.
  • Forest Range Officer / ACF and higher competitive posts: Age limit often ranges from 21 to 32 years (varies by recruitment), with relaxations for reserved categories.
  • Exact age limit, cut-off date and relaxation details always depend on the particular notification, so candidates should verify from the official advertisement before applying.

Forest Department Jobs Salary Overview

Forest department salary depends on the post, pay level, state and allowances. Senior officers under Indian Forest Service receive higher pay levels, while Forest Guard, Forester and similar posts fall under state government pay matrices.

Post Pay Level Basic Pay Gross Salary (Approx) Category
Forest Guard / Van Rakshak / Vana Palaka Level 3 ₹21,700 ₹28,000 – ₹35,000 Group C
Forester / Forest Sub-Inspector Level 6 ₹35,400 ₹45,000 – ₹56,000 Group C
Forest Beat Officer / Beat Guard / Junior Ranger Level 4 ₹25,500 ₹33,000 – ₹42,000 Group C
Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Ranger Level 10 ₹56,100 ₹70,000 – ₹90,000 Group B

Selection Process & Exam Pattern

The selection process for Forest Department Jobs 2026 typically includes written examinations, physical tests and document verification. Some posts also conduct medical examination and walking tests due to the demanding nature of field duties.

Written Exam, PET/PST & Document Verification

  • Written Exam: Most Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer recruitments start with a written exam based on objective-type questions (MCQs).
  • PET / PST (Physical Efficiency Test / Physical Standard Test): Candidates who qualify written exam are called for running, walking test, height, chest measurement and other physical parameters as per post and gender.
  • Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates must produce original certificates of age, qualification, caste, residence, sports/NCC and other documents mentioned in the notification.
  • Medical Examination: Many forest posts require medical fitness to ensure candidates can perform field duties in forests, wildlife areas and difficult terrain.

Syllabus Highlights for Forest Guard & Ranger

Typical syllabus for written exam may include:

  • General Knowledge & Current Affairs (national, state-level, environment and forest-related topics)
  • General Hindi / English or regional language (grammar, comprehension, vocabulary)
  • General Mathematics / Quantitative Aptitude (up to 10th or 12th level)
  • General Science and Environmental Studies (forest, wildlife, biodiversity, conservation, climate, basic biology)
  • Reasoning Ability (logical and analytical reasoning)

Exact subject-wise marks, number of questions, negative marking and exam duration will be given in the respective forest department notification.

How to Apply for Forest Department Jobs 2026

Most Forest Department Jobs 2026 applications are accepted either online through official websites or offline via prescribed application forms. Candidates should carefully read the official notification before filling the form.

Online Application Process

  1. Visit the official website of the recruiting body (State Forest Department, State PSC, Staff Selection Board or other government portal).
  2. Find the latest link for Forest Guard / Forester / Forest Range Officer / other forest posts and open the detailed advertisement.
  3. Complete the registration process with basic details like name, mobile number, email ID and verify using OTP if required.
  4. Fill the online application form with personal, educational, address and category information exactly as per documents.
  5. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature and other certificates in the prescribed format and size.
  6. Pay the application fee online through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card or UPI as per your convenience.
  7. Finally, submit the form and take a printout or PDF of the submitted application and payment receipt for future reference.

Documents Required & Application Fee

  • Recent passport size photograph and signature (scanned)
  • Class 10th and 12th mark sheets and certificates
  • Degree / Diploma certificates for graduate-level posts
  • Caste / Category certificate (SC, ST, OBC, EWS, etc.)
  • Domicile / Residence certificate if required by the state
  • NCC, Sports, Ex-serviceman and other quota certificates if applicable
  • Valid ID proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN, Voter ID, Driving Licence, Passport, etc.)

Why Choose Forest Department Jobs?

Forest department jobs are popular among candidates who love nature, wildlife and field work. These jobs offer job security, government benefits and a chance to work in forest conservation, wildlife protection, eco-tourism and community development.

  • Stable government job with regular salary, pension/NPS and allowances
  • Opportunity to work in forests, wildlife sanctuaries, national parks and protected areas
  • Contribution to environment protection, afforestation and biodiversity conservation
  • Good promotion avenues from Forest Guard to Forester, Forest Range Officer and higher posts in many states.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The AIIMS NORCET 10 City Intimation Slip 2026 is released on 4th April 2026 by the All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), New Delhi. This slip provides candidates with advance information about their allotted exam city for Stage-I (Preliminary) Exam to help with travel and accommodation planning. It is not the admit card. The AIIMS NORCET 10 Admit Card 2026 will be released on 8th April 2026.

Key Highlights

  • Release Date: 4th April 2026 (Released)
  • Stage-I (Prelims) Exam Date: 11th April 2026 (Saturday, Computer Based Test)
  • Stage-II (Mains) Exam Date: 30th April 2026
  • Exam Mode: Online Computer Based Test (CBT)
  • Total Vacancies: 2551 Nursing Officer Posts across various AIIMS institutions
  • Official Website: https://aiimsexams.ac.in/

AIIMS NORCET 10 City Intimation Slip 2026 - Quick Overview

Detail Information
Organization Name All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), New Delhi
Exam Name NORCET-10 (Nursing Officer Recruitment Common Eligibility Test) 2026
Post Name Nursing Officer
City Slip Release Date 4th April 2026 (Out)
Stage-I Exam Date 11th April 2026
Admit Card Release 8th April 2026
Exam Mode Computer Based Test (Online)
Total Vacancies 2551 Posts
Official Website aiimsexams.ac.in
Selection Process Stage-I (Prelims) → Stage-II (Mains) → Document Verification + Skill Test (if applicable)

Important Links

Resource Link / Action Status
Official Website https://aiimsexams.ac.in/ Active
NORCET-10 Candidate Login Login Portal (City Intimation Section) Active
Admit Card Download Available from 8th April 2026 Upcoming

Direct Download Process (Step-by-Step Guide):

  • Visit the official website: https://aiimsexams.ac.in/
  • Click on the Recruitments or NORCET-10 section.
  • Look for the link “NORCET-10 City Intimation Slip 2026” or “Information about City of Examination Centre”.
  • Click on the link and log in using your Candidate ID / Registration Number, Mobile Number, and Password (or Date of Birth as set during application).
  • Enter the captcha code if prompted.
  • Your City Intimation Slip will appear on the screen showing the allotted exam city.
  • Verify all details carefully, then download and save the PDF.
  • Take a printout for reference and keep a digital backup.

Tip: Download the slip as soon as possible to avoid server overload due to heavy traffic.

Key Information Available in City Intimation Slip

  • Candidate Name and Registration / Candidate ID
  • Allotted Exam City and City Code
  • Exam Date (11th April 2026)
  • Basic Exam Guidelines

Note: The slip only mentions the exam city. The complete exam centre address, roll number, reporting time, photo, signature, and detailed instructions will be available only in the Admit Card (releasing on 8th April 2026).

City Intimation Slip vs Admit Card - Key Differences

Aspect City Intimation Slip Admit Card / Hall Ticket
Purpose Advance info for travel planning Mandatory entry document
Release Date 4th April 2026 8th April 2026
Information Provided Exam city only Full centre address, roll no., photo, signature, shift timing, instructions
Allows Entry to Exam No Yes (with valid photo ID)
Contains Photo/Roll No. No Yes

📄 Complete Details & Notification

RBI Assistant Salary 2026 offers an attractive compensation package for entry-level positions in the Reserve Bank of India, featuring a revised starting basic pay, various allowances like Dearness Allowance and House Rent Allowance, and strong job security with growth potential.

The exact in-hand amount varies by city of posting, current DA rates, and individual deductions, but it remains one of the most competitive clerical roles in the banking sector.

RBI Assistant Salary

RBI Assistant Salary in 2026 is attractive for a clerical-level post, with a revised basic pay of Rs. 29,000 per month and gross salary going up to around Rs. 58,514 per month, depending on the structure considered.

The role offers not just a steady income but also a sense of security, perks, and a clear growth path, which is why it stays on the radar of serious banking aspirants every year. Many candidates describe their first RBI salary credit as that moment when all the mock tests, late-night revisions, and cancelled plans finally feel worth it.

For 2026, the RBI Assistant salary is governed by a revised pay scale starting at Rs. 29,000, with regular increments built into the structure over the years. The in-hand salary generally comes slightly lower than the gross figure after deductions like NPS, taxes, and other small contributions, but it still remains competitive compared to most entry-level banking jobs.

The mix of respectable pay, a 5-day work week in RBI, and a more stable workload than many frontline private bank roles often makes this job feel like a long-term, sustainable career choice rather than just a stepping stone.

The RBI Assistant 2026 notification was released on February 16, 2026, for 650 vacancies (Panel Year 2025), with applications open from February 16 to March 8, 2026.

Reserve Bank of India RBI Assistant Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - APPLY NOW

RBI Assistant Salary Structure

RBI Assistant Salary structure in 2026 revolves around a revised basic pay of Rs. 29,000 and multiple allowances such as DA, HRA, grade allowance, and transport allowance added on top of it. Instead of looking at the salary as just one big figure, it helps to break it down into components, because that’s exactly what appears on the salary slip and what candidates usually Google right after getting selected.

As per the updated details, one structure shows a gross salary of Rs. 58,514 per month, depending on the combination of allowances considered. This difference mainly arises due to variation in specific allowance heads like additional pay, grade allowance, DA, and transport allowance.

Still, both point in the same direction: a solid entry-level banking salary where the fixed monthly earnings feel reliable enough to plan EMIs, savings, and the occasional weekend splurge.

RBI Assistant Pay Scale (as per latest pattern) The official pay scale is: ₹29,000 – 1700 (3) – 34,100 – 2040 (4) – 42,260 – 2720 (6) – 58,580 – 2950 (2) – 64,480 – 3370 (3) – 74,590 – 4050 (1) – 78,640.

  • Starting Basic Pay: ₹29,000 per month.
  • Increment Pattern: Annual increments as per the scale stages (e.g., ₹1,700 for first three years, then ₹2,040, etc.).
  • Maximum Basic Pay (end of scale): ₹78,640 (after years of service and increments).

This represents a significant revision from previous structures, enhancing entry-level earnings.

RBI Assistant Gross Salary - Component-Wise

The gross monthly emoluments (approximate, excluding HRA in some calculations) stand at around ₹58,514, including various allowances. Components are variable based on RBI rules, city classification, and current rates.

In practice, the exact figure may vary slightly with posting location and internal policies, but candidates can safely expect the gross to fall in this bracket when joining as RBI Assistant in 2026.

Check: RBI Assistant Syllabus and Exam Pattern 2026

RBI Assistant In-Hand Salary 2026 (After Deductions)

The in-hand (net) salary is the gross amount minus statutory and other deductions. Estimates range from approximately ₹48,000 to ₹55,000 per month for new joiners, depending on city, tax regime, and exact deductions.
Common Deductions

  • Provident Fund (PF) / National Pension System (NPS) contribution

  • Income Tax (TDS) – varies by individual slab/regime

  • Professional Tax (state-specific)

  • Other minor contributions (e.g., staff welfare, if applicable)

Net pay varies: higher in metros due to HRA but may include more tax; actual take-home is typically ₹50,000–₹54,000 in many cases (based on recent patterns). Individual factors like tax-saving investments can influence the final amount.

RBI Assistant Salary Deductions

RBI Assistant Salary deductions in 2026 mainly include contributions towards pension (like NPS or PF), professional tax, and a few small recurring deductions such as meal coupons or membership fees.
These are the things that make candidates think, “Why is the bank paying less than what the notification promised?” but they are standard for most government-linked jobs.

One detailed breakup shows that around Rs. 6,576 is deducted from the gross RBI Assistant salary every month under different heads.

This deduction brings the net in-hand salary slightly down from the gross number but still keeps it attractive for a fresher-level banking role. Most of these deductions are either statutory or linked to long-term benefits, so they quietly support future financial security even if they pinch a little in the first few months.

Deduction Head

After these deductions, the in-hand salary often settles around the Rs. 52,000 – 55,000 range, depending on city category and specific structure.

RBI Assistant Allowances & Benefits 2026

Beyond salary, RBI Assistants enjoy comprehensive perks that add significant value:

  • House Rent Allowance (HRA): Paid if bank accommodation not provided; varies by city class (higher in X-class metros).

  • Dearness Allowance (DA): Inflation-linked; revised periodically for neutralisation.

  • Transport Allowance: Fixed support for commuting.

  • Medical Benefits: Reimbursement/coverage for self and dependents.

  • Leave & LTC: Eligible for leave travel concession and other leaves.

  • Retirement Benefits: Pension/gratuity under applicable rules, plus concessional loans and education allowances in some cases.

These benefits make the overall package highly rewarding compared to similar clerical roles.

RBI Assistant Job Profile

RBI Assistants handle clerical and support tasks in branches or offices. Daily work typically includes:

  • Data entry and record maintenance.

  • Assisting with customer queries and transactions.

  • Processing RTGS/NEFT/NEFT-related work.

  • Handling files, correspondence, and basic accounts.

  • Supporting departments like banking operations or currency management.

Office hours are generally standard (around 9–5), with reasonable work pressure compared to some commercial banks. It offers good work-life balance, making it a stable central banking career choice.

RBI Assistant Salary Slip

RBI Assistant salary slip is a monthly document that shows the detailed breakup of earnings and deductions, basic pay, allowances, contributions, and the final in-hand amount. It is the most realistic snapshot of what the RBI Assistant salary 2026 actually looks like in practice.

Typically, the slip will separately list basic pay, DA, HRA, transport allowance, grade allowance, special or local compensatory allowances, and then all deductions like NPS/PF, professional tax, and any other contributions. 

The final line shows the net salary credited to the account, which many candidates take a screenshot of and send to family or close friends the first time it arrives.

Even though sample slips may differ slightly between offices or states, the structure remains consistent and transparent, helping employees track their growth, understand tax slabs, and plan things like investments, insurance, or home loans.

Eventually, salary slips also become important proof for visa applications, loan processing, and future job switches, making them more than just a monthly PDF in the inbox.

RBI Assistant Salary in Different Cities / Posting Locations

Salary varies mainly due to HRA and CCA, which depend on whether the posting is in a metro, major city, or smaller center. Metro postings (like Mumbai, Delhi) often yield higher in-hand due to elevated allowances, while non-metro spots might be slightly lower but come with lower living costs.

RBI Assistant Salary Benefits

RBI Assistant salary benefits in 2026 go beyond the monthly cash component and include a wide range of perks, allowances, and facilities that add strong non-monetary value. For many employees, these benefits like accommodation, medical support, and concessional loans feel almost as important as the salary itself.

Some of the key benefits include:

  • Bank accommodation, subject to availability, with a very nominal license fee if allotted.

  • Reimbursement for vehicle maintenance used for official purposes.

  • Support for newspaper, briefcase, book grants, and furnishing of residence, as per eligibility.

  • Medical facilities provide dispensary support and reimbursement for OPD and hospitalization expenses.

  • Interest-free festival advances and Leave Fare Concession (LFC) for travel.

  • Concessional loans for housing, car, education, consumer goods, and even personal computers, after completing the required minimum service period.

  • Coverage under a defined contribution pension scheme, along with gratuity benefits.

There is also the intangible benefit of a 5-day work week in RBI and a more predictable work-life balance than many other banking jobs, which often becomes a deciding factor for people who value time with family or personal projects outside office hours.

Career Growth for RBI Assistant

Career growth for RBI Assistant in 2026 is structured yet rewarding, with clear paths to reach officer-level posts through internal exams and experience-based promotions. This is one of the main reasons many aspirants choose RBI Assistant over some private sector roles that may offer slightly higher initial pay but less predictable growth.

After completing the minimum required years of service usually about 2–3 years RBI Assistants become eligible to appear for departmental promotion processes. There are typically two main routes:

  • A normal process, where candidates clear a written test and are promoted based on seniority and performance.

  • A merit-based route, where additional qualifications like JAIIB and CAIIB along with experience play a big role.

Over time, an RBI Assistant can climb to posts such as Assistant Manager (Grade A), Manager (Grade B), Senior Manager (Grade C), and Chief Manager (Grade D), depending on performance and vacancies. 

Many long-term employees recall how the first promotion felt like a turning point: not just financially, but also in terms of confidence and responsibility. With consistent effort, exam preparation, and on-the-job learning, the RBI Assistant role can evolve from an entry point into a full-fledged central banking career.

RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026 OUT - Download Hall Ticket at rbi.org.in

RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026 has been released by the Reserve Bank of India on 2 April 2026. Candidates who applied for the Assistant recruitment (Panel Year 2025) can download their admit card from the official website opportunities.rbi.org.in or through the IBPS portal. The online preliminary examination is scheduled for 11 April 2026 and 13 April 2026 across various centers. This hall ticket is mandatory for appearing in the exam. Read below for the direct download link, step-by-step process, exam details, and important instructions for RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026.

How to Download RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026?

Follow these simple steps to download your RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026:

  1. Step 1: Visit the official RBI website at www.rbi.org.in or go directly to opportunities.rbi.org.in
  2. Step 2: Navigate to the “Opportunities at RBI” or “Current Vacancies” section
  3. Step 3: Click on “Call Letters” or “Recruitment for the Post of Assistant – Panel Year 2025”
  4. Step 4: Select “Download Call Letter for Online Preliminary Examination”
  5. Step 5: Enter your credentials:
    • Registration Number / Roll Number
    • Password / Date of Birth
    • Captcha Code (if required)
  6. Step 6: Click on “Submit” button
  7. Step 7: Your RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026 will appear on screen
  8. Step 8: Download and save the PDF file on your device
  9. Step 9: Take printout on A4 sheet with clear photograph

Pro Tip: Check all details on your admit card immediately after download. Report any discrepancies to RBI helpdesk without delay.

RBI Assistant 2026 - Important Dates

Event Date Status
Application Start Date 16 February 2026 Completed
Application Last Date 10 March 2026 Completed
Correction Window 14-15 March 2026 Completed
Admit Card Release Date 2 April 2026 Live Now
Prelims Exam Date 11 & 13 April 2026 Upcoming
Mains Exam Date 7 June 2026 (Tentative) Awaited
Result Declaration To be announced Awaited

Details on RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026

Your RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026 will contain the following information:

Candidate Information:

  • Candidate Name
  • Roll Number / Registration Number
  • Date of Birth
  • Category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS)
  • Gender
  • Candidate’s Photograph and Signature

Exam Information:

  • Exam Name: Assistant Prelims Online Exam
  • Exam Date & Shift/Timing
  • Reporting Time & Gate Closing Time
  • Exam Center Name and Complete Address
  • Language of Test

Important Instructions:

  • Documents to carry on exam day
  • Items allowed and not allowed in examination hall
  • Guidelines for PwBD candidates

Documents Required on RBI Assistant Prelims Exam Day

Mandatory Documents (Originals):

  • RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026 (printed copy with clear photograph)
  • Valid Photo ID Proof (any one of the following):
    • Aadhaar Card (with photo)
    • PAN Card
    • Passport
    • Driving License
    • Voter’s ID Card
    • Bank Passbook with Photograph
    • Employee ID / College ID
  • Recent passport-size photographs (if required)

Not Allowed in Examination Hall:

  • Mobile phones, smartwatches, Bluetooth devices
  • Calculators and electronic gadgets
  • Books, notes, study materials
  • Bags, purses, wallets

Note: Carrying prohibited items may lead to disqualification. Ration Card and Learner’s Driving License are not valid ID proofs.

Important Instructions for RBI Assistant Prelims Exam 2026

Before Exam Day:

  • Download admit card immediately and take multiple printouts
  • Verify exam center location and plan your travel
  • Check all details on the admit card for any errors
  • Read the Information Handout carefully

On Exam Day:

  • Reach exam center at least 45-60 minutes before reporting time
  • Entry will close as per gate closing time mentioned on admit card
  • Carry only allowed items; no safekeeping facility for prohibited items
  • Follow all COVID or other guidelines if mentioned

During Exam:

  • Negative marking of 0.25 for each wrong answer
  • Follow invigilator instructions strictly
  • Manage time for all sections
  • Do not carry any electronic devices inside the hall

What If RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card Not Available?

If you’re unable to download your RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026, try these solutions:

  1. Solution 1: Clear browser cache and cookies, try in incognito mode
  2. Solution 2: Use a different browser (Chrome recommended)
  3. Solution 3: Check your registration details and ensure application was successful
  4. Solution 4: Contact RBI helpdesk or IBPS grievance portal immediately

Important: Resolve any download issues well before the exam date. Keep checking the official website for updates.

Stay Updated: Bookmark official RBI website for latest updates on RBI Assistant Mains Admit Card 2026, result, and all recruitment notifications.

Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Selection Process 2026

The Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026 selection process consists of multiple stages such as shortlisting, online assessment, psychometric evaluation, and personal interview. These stages are designed to select the most suitable candidates for wealth management roles.

Shortlisting & Application Scrutiny:

In the initial stage, applications will be scrutinised based on eligibility criteria, educational qualifications, relevant work experience, domain expertise, and other parameters decided by the bank. Only candidates shortlisted as per the bank’s internal norms will be called for further stages. Mere fulfilment of eligibility criteria does not guarantee shortlisting for the Bank of Baroda WMS selection process.

Online Test & Psychometric Assessment:

The selection process may include an online test followed by a psychometric assessment or other suitable evaluations. These tests are conducted to assess the candidate’s personality, communication skills, problem-solving ability, efficiency, innovation, and willingness to work anywhere in India. The bank reserves the right to conduct or waive the online test and/or psychometric test depending on recruitment requirements.

Personal Interview (PI):

Candidates shortlisted from earlier stages will be invited for a Personal Interview. During the interview, candidates will be evaluated on domain knowledge, relationship management skills, leadership qualities (especially for higher roles), communication skills, and overall suitability for wealth management positions. Candidates must secure a minimum of 60% marks (55% for reserved categories) or as decided by the bank.

Final Merit & Provisional Selection:

The final merit list will be prepared based on the candidate’s performance in the online test (if conducted), psychometric test, and personal interview, as per the weightage decided by the bank. Only those candidates who qualify in all stages and rank high in the merit list, subject to vacancy and category norms, will be provisionally selected. The decision of the bank in all matters related to selection will be final and binding.

How to Apply for Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026

Interested and eligible candidates must apply online through the official website of Bank of Baroda within the specified dates. No other mode of application will be accepted.

Step 1 – Visit Official Website:

Visit the official website and navigate to the “Careers → Current Opportunities” section. Then click on the link for Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026 (Advt. No. BOB/HRM/REC/ADVT/2026/08).

Step 2 – Read Detailed Notification:

Download and carefully read the official notification PDF to understand vacancy details, eligibility criteria, age limit, experience requirements, selection process, application fee, and instructions.

Step 3 – Online Registration:

Click on “Apply Online” and complete the registration by entering basic details such as name, mobile number, and email ID. Generate your registration ID and password. Ensure your contact details remain active throughout the recruitment process.

Step 4 – Fill Online Application Form:

Log in using your credentials and fill out the application form with accurate personal, educational, and professional details. Verify all information carefully before final submission, as no corrections will be allowed afterward.

Step 5 – Upload Photograph, Signature & Documents:

Upload a recent passport-size photograph, signature, and required documents such as educational certificates, experience letters, and category certificates in the prescribed format and size.

Step 6 – Pay Application Fee Online:

Pay the application fee through available online modes such as debit card, credit card, net banking, or UPI. After successful payment, download the e-receipt and application form.

Step 7 – Final Submission & Print:

Submit the application form and take a printout of the final submitted form and fee receipt for future reference, especially for interview and document verification.

General Instructions for Bank of Baroda WMS Recruitment 2026

Applicants for Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026 must adhere to all general instructions issued by the bank to ensure smooth processing of their applications and avoid disqualification at any stage.

Single Valid Application: Candidates should submit only one valid online application for a post; in case of multiple applications, only the latest complete application will be considered and all fees paid for previous registrations will stand forfeited. Multiple appearances for a single post in the selection process will lead to cancellation of candidature.

Accuracy of Information: All information furnished in the Bank of Baroda WMS online application form 2026 must be true, complete and correct. If any discrepancy, false information, or suppression of material facts is found at any stage (before or after appointment), the candidate’s candidature will be cancelled and, if already engaged, services may be terminated without notice.

Document Verification & Category Proof: Candidates must produce original documents (age proof, educational certificates, experience proofs, category certificates for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwD, NOC from employer, etc.) along with self‑attested photocopies at the time of interview or whenever called by the bank. No change of category will be permitted after online registration, and results will be processed based on the category declared in the application.

Email & Communication: All communication regarding Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026 including call letters, schedule of tests/interviews, and any updates will be sent to the registered email ID and/or published on the Bank of Baroda website. The bank will not be responsible for non‑receipt of email due to incorrect email ID, spam filtering or technical issues.

Eligibility & Disqualification: Candidates are responsible for ensuring that they meet the eligibility criteria regarding nationality, age, qualification, experience and credit history. Applications from candidates with unsatisfactory credit reports, pending or adverse disciplinary/criminal records, or those not meeting the criteria at any stage are liable to be rejected and no correspondence in this regard will be entertained.

Jurisdiction & Bank’s Decision: Any dispute related to BOB WMS Recruitment 2026 will fall under the jurisdiction of courts/tribunals in Mumbai only. The decisions of Bank of Baroda regarding shortlisting, selection, eligibility, conduct of online test/PI and all other aspects of the recruitment process will be final, binding and not open to challenge by candidates.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

U.P. Co-operative Institutional Service Board, Lucknow has released the UP Co-operative Bank Special Recruitment 2026 short notification for Combined Co-operative Banking and Technical Services Examination to fill a total of 116 vacancies for Manager, Junior Manager, Assistant/Cashier (Banking Assistant/Clerical Staff), Assistant/Typist and Assistant Engineer (Civil) posts in District Co-operative Banks and U.P. State Construction Co-operative Federation across Uttar Pradesh.

This UP Co-operative Bank Recruitment 2026 special drive is exclusively for candidates belonging to Scheduled Caste and Other Backward Classes of Uttar Pradesh who are preparing for latest Uttar Pradesh govt bank jobs, with online applications to be submitted through the official board website within the prescribed dates.

UP Co-operative Bank Special Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Notification PDF for UP Co-operative Bank Special Recruitment 2026 (Combined Co-operative Banking and Technical Services Examination – 2026) is available on the official website of U.P. Co-operative Institutional Service Board, Lucknow, under the recruitment section for special drive vacancies in District Co-operative Banks and U.P. State Construction Co-operative Federation Ltd.

Eligibility Criteria – UP Co-operative Bank Special Recruitment 2026

Candidates must fulfil the eligibility criteria regarding age, educational qualification as on the cut-off date 01 January 2026.

Age Limit (as on 01.01.2026)

  • Minimum Age: 21 years
  • Maximum Age: 40 years (should not have been born before 01.01.1986 and not later than 01.01.2005)
  • Divyangjan (PwD) of Uttar Pradesh: Maximum 55 years
  • Upper age relaxation for SC, OBC, Ex-Servicemen, Skilled Sportspersons, State Employees etc. as per U.P. Government rules.

Educational Qualification & Other Requirements

  • Manager / Junior Manager (Category-1 & 2): Bachelor’s Degree in Commerce / Economics / Mathematics / Statistics with minimum 55% marks OR B.Tech/B.E. (CS/IT) / BCA / MCA / BBA / MBA (Banking/Finance/HR) / PGDM (Banking/Finance/HR) with minimum 55% marks + ‘O’ Level Computer certificate (or equivalent as per U.P. Govt. mandate dated 05.05.2022).
  • Assistant/Cashier (Banking Assistant/Clerical Staff) (Category-3): Bachelor’s Degree with minimum 55% marks + CCC certificate in Computer.
  • Assistant/Typist (Category-3): Graduation + Typing speed 30 wpm (Hindi) & 40 wpm (English) + CCC certificate in Computer.
  • Assistant Engineer (Civil) (Category-2): Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering or equivalent from a recognised University/Institution.
  • Only candidates belonging to Scheduled Castes and Other Backward Classes of Uttar Pradesh are eligible to apply.

Selection Process - UP Co-operative Bank Special Recruitment 2026

The selection will be based on:

Stage 1 – Online Examination

  • Preliminary Examination (if number of applications is large) – 100 marks
  • Main Examination – 200 marks

Stage 2 – Interview / Skill Test / Proficiency Test (as applicable)

Stage 3 – Document Verification

Final selection will be made on the basis of marks obtained in Main Examination + Interview / Skill Test (wherever applicable) subject to document verification and fulfilment of all eligibility conditions.

Application Fee - UP Co-operative Bank Special Recruitment 2026

Category Examination Fee
OBC of Uttar Pradesh ₹500/-
SC of Uttar Pradesh ₹250/-
Divyangjan of Uttar Pradesh ₹0/- (Nil)

Note: Bank transaction charges will be borne by the candidate. Fee once paid is non-refundable. If examination is conducted in two stages (Prelims + Mains), fee will be payable separately for both stages.

UP Co-operative Bank Application Form 2026

The online registration for UP Co-operative Bank Special Recruitment 2026 for 116 vacancies will be active from 25 March 2026 to 15 April 2026. Interested SC and OBC candidates of Uttar Pradesh must visit the official portal of the U.P. Co-operative Institutional Service Board and fill the UP Co-operative Bank Application Form 2026 by providing accurate details, uploading documents and paying the examination fee within the deadline to be considered for the Combined Co-operative Banking and Technical Services Examination 2026.

How to Apply - UP Co-operative Bank Recruitment 2026

The online application process for UP Co-operative Bank Special Recruitment 2026 will start from 25 March 2026 and the last date to submit the UP Co-operative Bank online application form is 15 April 2026. Candidates must visit the official website of U.P. Co-operative Institutional Service Board (upcisb.org / upcislb.upsdcb.gov.in as per notice), register themselves and complete their UP Co-operative Bank Application Form 2026 with correct personal, educational and category details before the last date.

  • Step 1: Visit the official website https://www.upcisb.upsdc.gov.in and click on “APPLY ONLINE”.
  • Step 2: Click on “Click here for New Registration” and fill basic details to generate Registration Number & Password.
  • Step 3: Login and fill the complete online application form carefully.
  • Step 4: Upload scanned Photo, Signature, Left Thumb Impression and Handwritten Declaration as per specifications.
  • Step 5: Pay the required application fee online.
  • Step 6: Preview, verify all details and submit the application.
  • Step 7: Take printout of the final submitted application form and e-Receipt for future reference.

Candidates can apply for only one post/group. Multiple applications will be rejected.

How to Apply for RNSB Recruitment 2026

Candidates can submit their applications for RNSB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 only in online mode through the official website of Rajkot Nagarik Sahakari Bank.

  • Visit the official website of RNSB: rnsb.bank.in.

Selected candidates will be appointed on probation for a period of two years as Assistant Grade-II (Clerk/Cashier) on a pay scale of 21,346-1386-10-35206-EB-1572-15-58786.

Security Deposit: Selected candidates will be required to submit a refundable security amount of Rs. 50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand only) at the time of joining service.

KNSB Assistant Grade-II Recruitment 2026 – Application Fee

Category Fee
All Candidates Rs. 1,000/- (inclusive of GST)

Fee is non-refundable. Payment can be made online through Debit Cards (RuPay/Visa/MasterCard/Maestro), Credit Cards, Internet Banking, IMPS, Cash Cards, or Mobile Wallets. Bank transaction charges will have to be borne by the candidate.

KNSB Assistant Grade-II Recruitment 2026 – Examination Scheme

The Online Written Examination will consist of 5 objective tests for a total of 200 marks and a duration of 145 minutes. There is a penalty of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer. Each question has 5 answer choices.

Sr. No. Name of Test Questions Marks Duration
1 Reasoning 40 40 35 Minutes
2 English Language 40 40 35 Minutes
3 General Awareness (with special reference to Banking) 40 40 20 Minutes
4 Computer Knowledge 40 40 20 Minutes
5 Quantitative Aptitude 40 40 35 Minutes
  Total 200 200 145 Minutes

Penalty: 0.25 marks deducted for each wrong answer. Cutoffs are applied at two stages: on individual test scores and on total score.

Exam Centres:

Sr. No. Centre
1 Haldwani (Distt. Nainital)
2 Dehradun (Distt. Dehradun)

Choice of centre once exercised will be final. No request for change of centre/venue/date will be entertained.

KNSB Assistant Grade-II Recruitment 2026 – Selection Process

Stage 1 – Online Written Examination: Candidates who qualify the Online Written Test will be considered for selection. Selection will be purely on merit basis based on marks obtained in the Online Written Test, conducted through IBPS.

Stage 2 – Medical Fitness: Appointment letters will be issued on merit basis subject to candidates being found medically fit for the assignment.

The Bank reserves the right to reject any application without assigning any reason. No correspondence in this regard will be entertained.

How to Apply for KNSB Assistant Grade-II Recruitment 2026

Step 1 – Before applying, scan and keep ready your Photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm, 20–50 KB), Signature (black ink on white paper, 10–20 KB), Left Thumb Impression (black/blue ink on white paper, 20–50 KB), and a Handwritten Declaration (black ink on white paper, 50–100 KB) in JPG/JPEG format.

Step 2 – Visit the bank's official website at kurmanchalbank.bank.in, click on "KNSB CARES" → "CAREER" → "APPLY NOW".

Step 3 – Click on "Click here for New Registration" and enter your Name, Contact details, and Email ID. Note down the Provisional Registration Number and Password received on your email/SMS.

Step 4 – Fill in all required personal, educational, and other details in the online application form carefully. Use "SAVE AND NEXT" to save progress.

Step 5 – Upload Photograph, Signature, Left Thumb Impression, and Handwritten Declaration as per the prescribed specifications.

Step 6 – Preview the complete application form. Click "COMPLETE REGISTRATION" only after verifying all details. No changes will be allowed after this.

Step 7 – Proceed to the Payment tab and pay the application fee of Rs. 1,000/- online. Wait for confirmation — do not press Back or Refresh to avoid double charge. Take a printout of the e-Receipt.

Step 8 – Download and take a printout of the system-generated online application form for future reference.

Step 9 – Download Call Letter from kurmanchalbank.bank.in when notified via email/SMS before the examination.

Documents to Carry at Examination Centre

  • Printout of Call Letter with recent passport-size photograph affixed
  • Currently valid Photo Identity Proof (original + photocopy): Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Passport, Permanent Driving License, Voter's Card, or valid College/University ID Card with photo
  • Note: Ration Card and Learner's Driving License are NOT valid ID proof
  • Name on Call Letter must exactly match the name on Photo Identity Proof

Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) under the Ministry of Railways have issued the Centralised Employment Notification RRB CEN 01/2026 for the recruitment of 11,127 Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP) posts in Level 2 of the 7th CPC with an initial pay of ₹19,900 across all Indian Railways.

This bumper railway vacancy is a major opportunity for candidates preparing for latest Railway Govt Jobs as well as state-wise railway jobs, because applications for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 can be submitted online for any participating RRB including Ahmedabad, Chennai, Kolkata, Mumbai, Secunderabad and others.

Eligible candidates aged 18 to 30 years as on 01 July 2026 can apply for RRB Assistant Loco Pilot posts through online mode only from 15 May 2026 to 14 June 2026 (23:59 hrs) by visiting the official recruitment portal rrbapply.gov.in and the respective RRB websites mentioned in CEN 01/2026.

Age Limit 18 to 30 Years (as on 01.07.2026)
Start Date for Apply 15-05-2026
Last Date for Apply 14-06-2026 (23:59 Hrs)
Official Website rrbapply.gov.in

📄 Complete Details & Notification

South Central Railway has released the South Central Railway Apprentice Recruitment 2025-26 notification for engagement of 2,801 Act Apprentices under the Apprentices Act 1961 and Apprenticeship Rules 1962 in various trades like Fitter, Electrician, Welder, AC Mechanic, Carpenter, COPA, Diesel Mechanic and more. Eligible 10th pass ITI candidates belonging to the notified districts of Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu and Madhya Pradesh can apply online for SCR Act Apprentice 2025-26 from 12 March 2026 to 11 April 2026 through the RRC/SC page of scr.indianrailways.gov.in.

South Central Railway Apprentice Recruitment 2025-26 - Overview
Recruitment Name South Central Railway Act Apprentice Recruitment 2025-26
Organization South Central Railway (SCR), Railway Recruitment Cell, Secunderabad
Notification Number No. SCR/P-HQ/RRC/111/Act. App/2025-26 dated 11.03.2026
Post Name Act Apprentice (various trades under Apprentices Act, 1961)
Total Vacancies 2801 Act Apprentice posts
Trades AC Mechanic, Carpenter, COPA, Diesel Mechanic, Electrician, Electronic Mechanic, Fitter, Industrial Electronics, Machinist, MMTM, Motor Mechanic Vehicle, Painter, Welder
Training Units Workshops, Divisions and Sheds of Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, Personnel and S&T Departments under SCR (e.g., Lallaguda, Rayanapadu, Tirupati, Secunderabad, Hyderabad, Vijayawada, Guntakal, Guntur, Nanded etc.)
Training Year Apprenticeship Training Programme for the year 2025-26
Educational Qualification 10th class (10+2 system) with minimum 50% aggregate marks and ITI in relevant trade from NCVT/SCVT recognised institute
Age Limit 15 to 24 years as on 11.04.2026 (upper age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC-NCL/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen as per rules)
Selection Process Merit list based on average of 10th marks and ITI marks, no written test or viva-voce
Application Mode Online only through RRC/SC webpage
Application Fee ₹100 for UR/OBC/EWS males; No fee for SC/ST, female/Transgender and PwBD candidates
Official Website https://scr.indianrailways.gov.in → About Us → RRC-SCR

South Central Railway Apprentice Recruitment 2025-26 Notification PDF

Notification PDF for South Central Railway Apprentice Recruitment 2025-26 is available on the RRC/SCR page of the South Central Railway website under the title “NOTIFICATION FOR ENGAGEMENT OF ACT APPRENTICES (2025-26) UNDER APPRENTICE ACT, 1961” with file number SCR/P-HQ/RRC/111/Act. App/2025-26 dated 11.03.2026.

Document Download Link
SCR Act Apprentice 2025-26 Official Notification PDF Download Notification PDF

South Central Railway Apprentice Recruitment 2025-26 Vacancy Details 

Trade Total
AC Mechanic 251
Carpenter 22
COPA 181
Diesel Mechanic 105
Electrician 1258
Electronic Mechanic 88
Fitter 539
Industrial Electronics 33
Machinist 54
MMTM 24

South Central Railway Apprentice Recruitment 2025-26 - Important Dates

Notification Release Date 11 March 2026
Online Application Start Date 12 March 2026, 17:00 hrs
Last Date to Apply Online 11 April 2026, 23:59 hrs
Cut-off Date for Age Calculation 11 April 2026
SCR Apprentice Merit List & Document Verification Dates To be notified on RRC/SCR website

Eligibility criteria - South Central Railway Act Apprentice 2025-26

1) Educational qualification​

  • 10th class/SSC passed under 10+2 system with minimum 50% aggregate marks from a recognised board.

  • ITI certificate in the relevant notified trade from an institute recognised by NCVT/SCVT (trade-wise: AC Mechanic, Carpenter, COPA, Diesel Mechanic, Electrician, Electronic Mechanic, Fitter, Industrial Electronics, Machinist, MMTM, Motor Mechanic Vehicle, Painter, Welder).

  • Candidate must possess required qualification as on date of online application submission.

  • Engineering graduates and diploma holders are not eligible for this Act Apprentice notification (covered under separate scheme).

2) Age limit (as on 11.04.2026)​

  • Minimum age: 15 years.

  • Maximum age: 24 years.

Category-wise permissible date of birth range:

Category Date of birth between
UR / EWS 11-04-2002 and 11-04-2011
SC / ST 11-04-1997 and 11-04-2011
OBC-NCL 11-04-1999 and 11-04-2011
Ex-Servicemen / PwBD (UR) 11-04-1992 and 11-04-2011
Ex-Servicemen / PwBD (SC/ST) 11-04-1987 and 11-04-2011
Ex-Servicemen / PwBD (OBC) 11-04-1989 and 11-04-2011

Relaxations included in above ranges:

  • SC/ST: +5 years.

  • OBC-NCL: +3 years.

  • PwBD: +10 years.

  • Ex-Servicemen: +10 years up to length of service in Defence Forces (no extra relaxation for their children).

3) Residential / jurisdiction eligibility​
Candidates must reside in districts under South Central Railway jurisdiction and provide valid proof of residence. Eligible areas:

  • Telangana: all districts.

  • Andhra Pradesh: all districts except Manyam, Srikakulam, Vizianagaram.

  • Maharashtra: Akola, Amaravati, Aurangabad, Beed, Chandrapur, Hingoli, Jalna, Latur, Nanded, Nasik, Parbhani, Washim, Yavatmal.

  • Karnataka: Balki, Bellari, Belgaum, Bidar, Gulbarga/Kalaburgi, Raichur, Yadgir.

  • Tamil Nadu: Vellore.

  • Madhya Pradesh: Burhanpur, Khandwa.

4) Category certificates (for reservation)​

  • SC/ST: Central Government format caste certificate (Annexure-B).

  • OBC-NCL: caste + NCL certificate (issued within 3 years) in Central format (Annexure-C) + self-declaration (Annexure-D).

  • EWS: valid Income & Asset certificate (Annexure-E).

  • PwBD: disability ≥40% in prescribed format (Annexure-G/H).

5) Salary/Stipend

  • Selected candidates will be paid a stipend during the apprenticeship at the prescribed rates as per extant instructions.

SCR Apprentice Selection Process 2025-26

  • Merit List Basis: Selection for SCR Act Apprentice 2025-26 will be done purely on merit, by taking the average of percentage marks in 10th (minimum 50% aggregate) and ITI marks, giving equal weightage to both.
  • No Exam / Interview: There will be no written test or viva-voce for South Central Railway Apprentice Recruitment 2025-26.
  • Tie-breaking: If two candidates have the same aggregate percentage, older candidate (higher age) will be preferred; if DOB is also same, the candidate who passed Matriculation earlier will be preferred.
  • Document Verification (DV): Shortlisted candidates will be called for DV where all original certificates (education, ITI, caste, EWS, PwBD, ExSM, RDAT registration, medical fitness) will be verified.
  • Medical Fitness: Candidates must produce medical fitness certificate from a government-authorised doctor (Gazetted, not below Asst. Surgeon) in prescribed format at DV; without this, they will not be considered.
  • Training & Stipend: Selected candidates will undergo apprenticeship training as per Central Apprenticeship Council syllabus and receive stipend at prescribed rates during training.
  • No Job Guarantee: SCR apprenticeship 2025-26 provides training only; there is no guarantee of regular railway job after completion of apprenticeship.

South Central Railway Apprentice Application Form 2026

  • Application Mode: Online only via RRC/SCR portal; no offline forms will be accepted.
  • Application Dates: SCR Apprentice online form 2026 is available from 12 March 2026 (17:00 hrs) to 11 April 2026 (23:59 hrs).
  • Fee: ₹100 for applicable categories; SC/ST, female/Transgender and PwBD candidates are exempt from fee payment.
  • Multiple Trades: Candidates with ITI in more than one trade can submit separate SCR Apprentice application 2026 for each trade.
  • Communication: All updates regarding SCR Act Apprentice 2025-26 application form, merit list and DV will be sent via registered email/SMS and uploaded on RRC-SCR webpage only.

How to Apply for South Central Railway Apprentice Recruitment 2025-26

  1. Visit the official South Central Railway website: https://scr.indianrailways.gov.in and go to About Us → RRC-SCR to open the ONLINE ACT APPRENTICE APPLICATION link.
  2. Read the detailed SCR Act Apprentice 2025-26 notification carefully and ensure you belong to the eligible states/districts and possess required 10th + ITI qualification.
  3. Complete Registration: select state and district, upload address proof, enter valid mobile number and email ID, create a password and verify OTPs sent to mobile and email.
  4. Login as a registered candidate and fill in personal details (name, DOB, category, etc.) and educational/technical details (10th marks, ITI trade, ITI marks, RDAT registration number).
  5. Select your priority/preference of training units (Divisions/Workshops/Sheds) only for trades you are eligible for; options once submitted cannot be changed.
  6. Upload a recent colour photograph (JPEG, ≤1 MB, not older than 3 months), signature image (≤1 MB) and required certificates (10th, ITI, RDAT form, caste, EWS, PwBD, ExSM, medical certificate) in PDF format (≤2 MB).
  7. Ensure you have completed RDAT/NAPS registration at www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in and obtained registration number before filling the SCR apprentice application.
  8. Check all details in the Preview page carefully; after clicking Submit, no modification will be allowed.
  9. Pay the application fee (if applicable) through online payment gateway (Net Banking/Debit Card/Credit Card/UPI) and generate e-receipt.
  10. Download/print the final submitted SCR Apprentice application form 2026 and keep it along with fee receipt and uploaded documents for Document Verification.

The Pune District Central Cooperative Bank (PDCC Bank) will officially publish the answer key for the Clerk recruitment exam 2025. Candidates who appear for the examination will be able to review the answer key once it is released. The recruitment exam for Clerk positions is scheduled to be held from 22nd to 25th August 2025. Through this recruitment drive, a total of 356 vacancies will be filled. Applicants will be advised to carefully review the answer key and submit any challenges within the stipulated timeframe, as no objections will be accepted after the deadline.

Candidates who appear for the exam will be able to access and download the provisional answer key by visiting the official website at pdccbank.co.in.

PDCC Bank Clerk Answer Key 2025 Will Be Released

Where To Check PDCC Bank Clerk Answer Key 2025?

On the official website, PDCC Bank will officially release the answer key for the Clerk Posts. Candidates who appear for the exam will be able to access and download the provisional answer key by visiting the official website at pdccbank.co.in.

UPPSC Staff Nurse Unani Answer Key 2026 – Download PDF, Response Sheet & Objection Link

The Uttar Pradesh Public Service Commission (UPPSC) has released the UPPSC Staff Nurse Unani Answer Key 2026 for the Preliminary Examination (Advt. No. A-3/E-1/2025, Staff Nurse Unani (Male/Female) (Pre.) Exam-2025). Candidates who appeared for the exam held on 17 March 2026 can now check and download the answer key along with question paper from the official website uppsc.up.nic.in.

This recruitment drive aims to fill 03 vacancies. If you find any discrepancy in the answers, you can raise objections before 25 March 2026.

How to Download UPPSC Staff Nurse Unani Answer Key 2026 ?

Follow these simple steps to download your answer key:

  1. Visit the official website: uppsc.up.nic.in
  2. Navigate to the "Answer Key" or "View Answer Key" section (or check latest notices)
  3. Click on "NOTICE REGARDING ADVT. NO. A-3/E-1/2025, STAFF NURSE UNANI (MALE/FEMALE)(PRE.) EXAM-2025" or related answer key link
  4. Log in using your Registration Number/Roll Number and Date of Birth/Password if required (or directly download PDF question paper & answer key)
  5. Your answer key with question paper will appear on screen
  6. Download and save the PDF for future reference

How to Raise Objection Against UPPSC Staff Nurse Unani Answer Key 2026 ?

If you disagree with any answer in the provisional key, follow these steps (as per UPPSC guidelines):

  1. Log in to your candidate portal on uppsc.up.nic.in
  2. Go to the "Objection" or "Challenge Answer Key" section (if available) or follow the notice instructions
  3. Select the question(s) you want to challenge
  4. Upload supporting documents or references (if required)
  5. Pay the objection fee (if applicable, details in official notice)
  6. Submit before the deadline: 25 March 2026

Important: Objections submitted after the deadline will NOT be considered. Refer to the official notice for exact procedure and fee details.

How to Calculate Your Score Using UPPSC Staff Nurse Unani Answer Key 2026 ?

Use this formula to estimate your score:

Total Score = (Correct Answers × Marks per Question) – (Wrong Answers × Negative Marking)

Marking Scheme for UPPSC Staff Nurse Unani Exam (Prelims):

  • Correct Answer: +1.33 marks (or as per official pattern, typically 100 questions for 150 marks)
  • Wrong Answer: -0.33 mark (negative marking, 1/3 deduction)
  • Unattempted: 0 marks
